Ayah

يَسْـَٔلُونَكَ عَنِ ٱلْأَنفَالِ ۖ قُلِ ٱلْأَنفَالُ لِلَّهِ وَٱلرَّسُولِ ۖ فَٱتَّقُوا۟ ٱللَّهَ وَأَصْلِحُوا۟ ذَاتَ بَيْنِكُمْ ۖ وَأَطِيعُوا۟ ٱللَّهَ وَرَسُولَهُۥٓ إِن كُنتُم مُّؤْمِنِينَ (۱)
They ask you concerning the anfaal. Say, ‘The anfaal belong to Allah and the Apostle.’ So be wary of Allah and settle your differences, and obey Allah and His Apostle, should you be faithful. (1)

Key Points

The word su'āl (question) and its derivatives appear about 130 times in the Qur'an, including the phrase "Yas'alūnaka" (They ask you) 15 times. Anfāl is the plural of nafl, meaning surplus or gift/grant (aṭiyyah). Just as supererogatory prayers (those exceeding the obligatory ones) are called nāfilah, the granting of a son to Prophet Abraham (عليه السلام) was also considered a nāfilah. "و وهبنا له اسحاق و یعقوب نافلة" (412) In narrations and jurisprudential books, natural resources and public wealth, war spoils (ghanā'im), ownerless properties such as land abandoned by its owner, the wealth of the deceased who have no heirs, forests, valleys, reed beds, barren lands (mawāt), mines, etc., are all considered part of Anfāl. (413) Since the method of distributing spoils before Islam was based on discrimination, after the Battle of Badr—which was the first war fought by the Muslims and involved significant spoils—there were various opinions regarding what should be done with the spoils, who should receive them, and who had priority. Finally, following the people's question and request to the Noble Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله, He personally undertook the distribution of the spoils and divided them justly and equally among the people, in order to eliminate the imaginary discriminations of the Age of Ignorance (Jāhiliyyah) and support the oppressed (mustaḍ'afīn), although some were displeased by this equality. (414) Although the verses of this Surah concern the Battle of Badr, and this verse primarily refers to war spoils, it is not exclusive to them. (415) Imam Sadiq عليه السلام said: Allah's share of the Anfāl is spent under the supervision of the Messenger of Allah صلى الله علیه وآله. (416) In narrations, the reward for reconciliation and making peace among people is mentioned as being greater than a year of supererogatory prayers and fasting. (417) Imam Sadiq عليه السلام told his student Mufaḍḍal: If a dispute arises between two of our followers, spend from my wealth and reconcile them. (418) In Islam, strengthening bonds and eliminating the causes of division and hypocrisy (nifāq) is so important that in some stages, not only is reconciliation (iṣlāḥ dhāt al-bayn) obligatory, but funds from the public treasury (Bayt al-Māl) may also be used for it. (419)

412) Anbiyā', 72 413) Tafsīr Nūr al-Thaqalayn; Biḥār, Vol. 93, p. 205 and Kāfī, Vol. 1, p. 539 414) Tafsīr Namūneh; Furūgh-e Abadiyyat, Vol. 1, p. 423 415) Tafsīr al-Mīzān and Namūneh 416) Tafsīr Namūneh 417) Tafsīr Aṭyab al-Bayān 418) Kāfī, Vol. 2, p. 209 419) Tafsīr Namūneh

Messages

  1. The people's question about Anfāl and the Prophet's صلى الله علیه وآله answer to them indicate Islam's involvement in the economic affairs of society. "یسئلونك..."
  2. The reference point and source for stating economic rulings and issues is the divine leader. "یسئلونك عن الانفال قل"
  3. The goal of Jihād (struggle) in Islam is the victory of truth over falsehood. Spoils are a secondary and supplementary matter. (Anfāl means "surplus.")
  4. The ownership of public wealth belongs to Allah; therefore, it must be placed in the hands of the most sacred and beloved of people. "قل الانفال للّه والرسول"
  5. The Islamic government requires economic support, and Anfāl is the support system for the Islamic order. "الانفال للّه والرسول"
  6. Islam also has laws governing natural resources and public wealth. "الانفال للّه و الرسول"
  7. All expenditures by the Messenger of Allah are within the scope of divine objectives. "للّه والرسول"
  8. Protecting public wealth requires purity and piety. "فاتّقوا اللّه" (Because the public treasury (Bayt al-Māl) and public wealth can be a breeding ground for misuse.)
  9. Whoever intends to bring reform to society must himself possess piety. "فاتّقوا اللّه و أصلحوا"
  10. Maintaining unity and reconciliation among people is incumbent upon everyone. "أصلحوا ذات بینكم"
  11. The believer must act according to divine commands in the fields of ethics, society, and politics in order to achieve legitimacy and success. "فاتّقوا اللّه و اصلحوا ذات بینكم و أطیعوا اللّه و رسوله"
  12. Faith is not only in the heart; it also requires external manifestation and practical obedience. "أطیعوا... ان كنتم مؤمنین"
  13. It is possible for some people to pass the test of self-sacrifice and presence on the battlefield, yet fail the test of wealth, the distribution of spoils, and Anfāl. "ان كنتم مؤمنین"
  14. Going to the front line alone is not a sign of complete faith; lack of greed for spoils, maintaining brotherhood, and submission to leadership are also necessary. (The entire verse)

Explanations

Portrait of Surah Al-Anfāl

This Surah has seventy-five verses and was revealed in Medina. It is named "Al-Anfāl" due to the word Anfāl appearing at its beginning, and also because it states the rulings concerning Anfāl and public wealth. Another name for this Surah is "Badr," because most of its verses revolve around the Battle of Badr, which was the first confrontation between the Muslims and the polytheists, and in which the Muslims benefited from divine aid and Allah's victory. In this Surah, alongside the discussion of the Battle of Badr, the characteristics of the army of truth versus the army of falsehood are subtly explained. Furthermore, lessons from the history of the Prophet of Islam, His conduct toward the Muslims, and issues such as Anfāl, the public treasury (Bayt al-Māl), the rulings of Jihād, spoils, captives, Khums (the fifth) and its distribution method, the story of the Hijra (migration) and Laylat al-Mabīt (the Night of Staying Over), the characteristics of true believers, the prevention of disagreement and division, etc., are discussed.

Ayahs

إِنَّمَا ٱلْمُؤْمِنُونَ ٱلَّذِينَ إِذَا ذُكِرَ ٱللَّهُ وَجِلَتْ قُلُوبُهُمْ وَإِذَا تُلِيَتْ عَلَيْهِمْ ءَايَـٰتُهُۥ زَادَتْهُمْ إِيمَـٰنًا وَعَلَىٰ رَبِّهِمْ يَتَوَكَّلُونَ (۲)
The faithful are only those whose hearts tremble [with awe] when Allah is mentioned, and when His signs are recited to them, they increase their faith, and who put their trust in their Lord, (2)
ٱلَّذِينَ يُقِيمُونَ ٱلصَّلَوٰةَ وَمِمَّا رَزَقْنَـٰهُمْ يُنفِقُونَ (۳)
maintain the prayer and spend out of what We have provided them. (3)

Key Points

In Ayah 2, Allah says: "اذا ذُكِر اللّه وَجلَت قلوبهم" (When Allah is mentioned, their hearts tremble), the remembrance of Allah makes the hearts of the believers anxious. But in another place, He says: "ألا بذكر اللّه تَطمئنّ القلوب" [420] (Verily, in the remembrance of Allah do hearts find rest), with the remembrance of Allah, hearts find peace. These two Ayahs do not contradict each other, because in one instance, the fear is due to the greatness of Allah, and in the other, it is confidence in Allah. Just as we read in another Ayah: "اللّه نَزّل احسن الحدیث كتاباً متشابهاً تَقشَعِرّ منه جلود الّذین یَخشون ربّهم ثمّ تلین جلودهم و قلوبهم الى ذكر اللّه" [421] (Allah has sent down the best statement, a Book consistent, whose recitation makes the skins of those who fear their Lord shiver, then their skins and their hearts soften to the remembrance of Allah). Those who have awe (khashyah) of Allah, their skin shivers upon reading and hearing the Quran, and after a while, they calm down and their hearts soften. Yes, the remembrance of Divine wrath and punishment makes the heart of the believer tremble, and the remembrance of Divine grace and mercy brings peace to his heart, just like a child who both fears his parents and is reassured by them. Wajal (وَجل) refers to the state of anxiety [422], dread, and fear in a person, which is sometimes due to the realization of responsibilities and the possibility of failing to perform duties, and sometimes due to the realization of the greatness of the Divine station and majesty. Therefore, we read in the Quran: "انّما یَخشَى اللّه من عباده العلماء" [423] (It is only those who have knowledge among His slaves that fear Allah). Only the knowledgeable and aware servants have awe (khashyah) of Allah. [424]

420) Ra‘d, 28 421) Zumar, 23 422) Al-Tahqiq fi Kalimat al-Quran 423) Fatir, 28 424) Tafsir Namuneh

Messages

1- Whoever remains indifferent upon hearing the call to prayer (Adhan) and the Divine verses should doubt the completeness of his faith. "انّما المؤمنون اذا ذُكر اللّه وَجِلَت"

2- The mention of Allah's Name, regardless of who makes it, affects the believer. "اذا ذُكر اللّه وَجِلَت"

3- Faith is accompanied by inner love and awe (khashyah). "المؤمنون... وَجِلت قلوبهم"

4- Faith has levels and degrees and is subject to decrease and increase. "زادتهم ایماناً"

5- Fear rooted in ignorance is bad, but fear stemming from knowledge is praiseworthy. "المؤمنون ... وجلت"

6- Every Ayah of the Quran is a proof, a reason, and a light that can increase faith. "اذا تلیت... زادتهم ایماناً"

7- The believer is between fear and hope. "المؤمنون، وجلت قلوبهم، یتوكّلون"

8- He who knows Allah alone as his Lord (Rabb) trusts only in Him. "على ربّهم یتوكّلون"

9- The sign of faith is that first, the believer's heart feels awe (khashyah) upon the remembrance of Allah, "وَجِلَت قلوبُهم," then his faith increases through the recitation and reminder of Divine verses, "زادتهم ایماناً," and he relies upon Allah, "یتوكّلون," establishes prayer, "یقیمون," and helps others. "ینفقون"

10- Human behavior stems from his motivations, perspectives, and beliefs. "انّما المؤمنون... یقیمون... ینفقون"

11- Islam has mandated the spending (infaq) of a portion of wealth and possessions, not all of it. "ممّا رزقناهم ینفقون" (One of the meanings of min (مِن) is partiality/a portion).

12- The prayer and spending of the believer are not temporary but continuous and permanent. "یقیمون... ینفقون"

13- Spending (infaq) must be from lawful wealth and Divine provision. "ممّا رزقناهم ینفقون"

14- The believer considers his possessions a Divine gift, not merely the product of his own labor, and this belief makes generosity and spending easy for him. "ممّا رزقناهم ینفقون"

Ayah

أُو۟لَـٰٓئِكَ هُمُ ٱلْمُؤْمِنُونَ حَقًّا ۚ لَّهُمْ دَرَجَـٰتٌ عِندَ رَبِّهِمْ وَمَغْفِرَةٌ وَرِزْقٌ كَرِيمٌ (٤)
It is they who are truly the faithful. They shall have ranks near their Lord, forgiveness and a noble provision. (4)

Key Points

"Rizq Karīm" (a generous provision) means a provision that is perpetual, without obligation (or reproach), vast, and pure.

Messages

1- Faith is complete when it is accompanied by the fear of Allah, reliance (Tawakkul), prayer, and spending (Infaq). Yes, faith is not achieved through slogans; it is achieved through action. (This follows the previous verses): «اولئك هم المؤمنون حقّاً»

2- The key to receiving divine ranks (Darajāt) is prayer and spending. «یقیمون... ینفقون، لهم درجات» (We strive our whole lives to attain worldly ranks, but we are heedless of the divine ranks!)

3- The ranks that Allah grants are unknown and extremely important to worldly human beings. (The word "Darajāt" is indefinite [Nakirah]).

4- Since people's faith is subject to increase and decrease, the divine ranks also increase or decrease. «زادتهم ایماناً... لهم درجات»

5- Divine ranks are not exclusive to Paradise; they also exist in this world and in the Barzakh (intermediate realm). («عند ربّهم» [with their Lord] is stated absolutely).

6- Even true believers are exposed to error and are in need of divine forgiveness (Maghfirah). «المؤمنون حقّاً لهم ... مغفرة»

7- Only true faith provides the basis for a person to reach a special status and receive forgiveness and the special bounties of Allah. «المؤمنون... عند ربّهم و مغفرة و رزق كریم»

Ayah

كَمَآ أَخْرَجَكَ رَبُّكَ مِنۢ بَيْتِكَ بِٱلْحَقِّ وَإِنَّ فَرِيقًا مِّنَ ٱلْمُؤْمِنِينَ لَكَـٰرِهُونَ (٥)
As your Lord brought you out from your home with a just cause, a part of the faithful were indeed reluctant. (5)

Key Points

Just as going to Jihad against the enemy in the Battle of Badr was difficult and burdensome for some due to the small number of personnel and resources, now too, the distribution of the spoils of that same war is burdensome for some. But these displeasures are not new and will pass. The Prophet of Allah must consider the true interest and carry out the command of Allah, otherwise, the aversion and displeasure of this person and that person will always exist.

Messages

1- The Prophet's صلى الله علیه وآله departure from Medina for the Battle of Badr was by the command of Allah and through His planning. «أخرجك ربّك»

2- Movement in the path of war and Jihad is a factor for growth and training. «اخرجك ربّك»

3- In the battlefronts, the main role belongs to the leader and commander. (Although a group departed for Jihad, the Ayah highlights the presence of the Prophet of Allah.) «أخرجك ربّك»

4- Medina is the home of the Holy Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله. (A person's home is where he resides or establishes his position, not just his birthplace.) «بیتك»

5- The leader must focus on fulfilling his duty and implementing the truth (Haqq). «بالحقّ»

6- Divine commands are based on truth and are for the purpose of reaching the truth. «اخرجك... بالحقّ»

7- Aversion and displeasure regarding Jihad, if not accompanied by disobedience and rebellion and if it does not harm obedience, does not damage the essence of faith. «من المؤمنین لكارهون»

8- Sometimes a person initially goes to the front with love and sincerity, but in the end, becomes upset and displeased due to financial issues and spoils. «لكارهون» (Based on the interpretation that the aversion relates to the manner of distributing the spoils.)

9- In social and financial matters, the existence of opposition and dissatisfaction is natural, even within a believing society and when a person like the Holy Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله is responsible for it. «فریقاً من المؤمنین لكارهون»

Ayah

يُجَـٰدِلُونَكَ فِى ٱلْحَقِّ بَعْدَ مَا تَبَيَّنَ كَأَنَّمَا يُسَاقُونَ إِلَى ٱلْمَوْتِ وَهُمْ يَنظُرُونَ (٦)
They disputed with you concerning the truth after it had become clear, as if they were being driven towards death as they looked on. (6)

Key Points

The dispute of a group of Muslims with the Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله, on the way to the Battle of Badr, was over the fact that they did not have sufficient resources and manpower, and that they had only set out to seize the property of the Quraysh merchants, not to fight the Quraysh army. Although they knew this reality was a command from Allah, they did not cease their objection; of course, some, like Miqdad, said: We will not sit back like the companions of Moses عليه السلام and say, 'You go and fight,' rather, we are people of war and we accept whatever you command. But some, out of fear and lack of readiness for war, argued with the Messenger of Allah صلى الله علیه وآله and considered the movement towards Jihad and the Battle of Badr as moving towards death. However, the subsequent events and the victory in the war showed that they were mistaken.

Dispute and argument aimed at denying the truth are rejected, and they have several types: A: Sometimes it occurs after the truth has become clear. «یجادلونك فى الحقّ بعد ما تبیّن» B: Sometimes it is not based on knowledge or awareness. «فلم تحاجّون فیما لیس لكم به علم» (425) C: Sometimes it is aimed at destroying the truth. «و یجادل الّذین كفروا بالباطل لیدحضوا به الحقّ» (426) Of course, sometimes discussion and argument are for the purpose of affirming the truth, which must be done in the best manner, and the best of them is the most excellent. «و جادلهم بالّتى هى احسن» (427)

425) آل عمران، 66 426) كهف، 56 427) نحل، 125

Messages

1- Not all companions of the Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله were obedient to His command. «یجادلونك»

2- Lazy and cowardly individuals always resort to dispute, justification, and excuse-making to evade the command of truth and Jihad. «یجادلونك فى الحقّ»

3- If spirits and motivations are not sound, knowing the truth alone is not effective. «بعد ما تبیّن»

4- Sometimes a person falls so low that even after recognizing the truth and the command of the truth, he still opposes it. «یجادلونك فى الحقّ بعد ما تبیّن»

5- The movement of soldiers weak in faith and low in morale towards battle is extremely difficult and accompanied by great fear and anxiety. «یُساقون الى الموت»

Ayah

وَإِذْ يَعِدُكُمُ ٱللَّهُ إِحْدَى ٱلطَّآئِفَتَيْنِ أَنَّهَا لَكُمْ وَتَوَدُّونَ أَنَّ غَيْرَ ذَاتِ ٱلشَّوْكَةِ تَكُونُ لَكُمْ وَيُرِيدُ ٱللَّهُ أَن يُحِقَّ ٱلْحَقَّ بِكَلِمَـٰتِهِۦ وَيَقْطَعَ دَابِرَ ٱلْكَـٰفِرِينَ (۷)
When Allah promised you [victory over] one of the two companies, [saying], ‘It is for you,’ you were eager that it should be the one that was unarmed. But Allah desires to confirm the truth with His words, and to root out the faithless, (7)

Key Points

Shawka (شوكة) derives from shawk (شوك), meaning thorn or spearhead, and symbolizes the "armed group." The intent of ghayr dhāt ash-shawka (غیر ذات الشوكة) here is the unarmed trade caravan. The intent of the "Words of Truth" (كلمات حقّ) is the divine traditions, divine commands, and the divine vicegerents (Awliyā'). The word dābir (دابر) means the rear or tail end of something, and yaqṭa‘u dābir (یقطع دابر) is a metaphor for eradication or uprooting.

In the month of Ramadan of the second year of the Hijra, the Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله was informed that Abu Sufyan was leading a very important trade caravan on its way to Mecca. To weaken the power of the disbelievers and compensate for the wealth that the disbelievers of Mecca had confiscated from the migrant Muslims, the Prophet, accompanied by 313 people, prepared to attack that trade caravan. Abu Sufyan, the leader of the caravan, upon learning of this decision, informed the disbelievers of Mecca and diverted the caravan to Mecca via a side route. Since many of the polytheists of Mecca had a share in that caravan, and also through their coercion and threats, about a thousand people were quickly mobilized. They set out, accompanied by the elders and notables of Mecca, fully equipped militarily under the command of Abu Jahl, to defend the forty-person group of traders.

On the seventeenth day of Ramadan, in a desert between Mecca and Medina, three groups were present: the Muslims, the army of disbelief, and the disbelievers' trade caravan. After being informed of the enemy army's movement, the Prophet consulted with his companions and followers: should they pursue the trade caravan or confront the army of disbelief? Several opinions were expressed, but they finally decided to confront the army of disbelief. Although the Muslims did not have much military equipment and had not set out with the intention of armed conflict, and the number of disbelievers was three times theirs, they ultimately triumphed over the enemy through the unseen aid of Allah. In this battle, Abu Jahl and seventy other disbelievers were killed—most of the casualties were at the hands of Ali عليه السلام—and seventy people were taken captive, while only 14 members of the army of Islam achieved martyrdom. [428]

428) Tafsir al-Mizan

Messages

1- Remembering divine aid is necessary and strengthens faith. (Wa idh)

2- Victory is not solely dependent on the number of personnel or military equipment; the main factor is the Will of Allah. (یرید اللّه - Yurīdu Allāhu). Yes, the Will of God prevails over natural factors.

3- Sometimes the divine Will is executed by the hands of the believers. (یرید اللّه - Yurīdu Allāhu)

4- Human nature seeks ease. (تَوَدّون أن غیر ذات الشوكة تكون لكم - You wished that the unarmed group would be yours). (We should not sacrifice fundamental and principled programs for simple and unprincipled matters due to hardship and difficulties.)

5- The establishment of truth (iḥqāq al-ḥaqq) is achieved through the divine Words (Awliyā', traditions, laws, commands, sacrifice, Jihad, and acting upon divine instructions). (یحقّ الحقّ بكلماته - He establishes the truth by His Words).

6- The triumph of truth over falsehood is superior to economic gain. (تَوَدّون أنّ غیر ذات الشوكة تكون لكم... یرید اللّه أن یحقّ الحقّ بكلماته - You wished that the unarmed group would be yours... but Allah intends to establish the truth by His Words).

7- The ultimate victory will be through the honor of truth and the humiliation of falsehood. (یرید اللّه ان یحقّ الحقّ... و یقطع دابر الكافرین - Allah intends to establish the truth... and cut off the root of the disbelievers).

8- The goal of Islamic Jihad is the establishment of truth and the destruction of falsehood, not territorial expansion or occupation. (أن یحقّ الحقّ بكلماته و یقطع دابر الكافرین - That He may establish the truth by His Words and cut off the root of the disbelievers).

Ayah

لِيُحِقَّ ٱلْحَقَّ وَيُبْطِلَ ٱلْبَـٰطِلَ وَلَوْ كَرِهَ ٱلْمُجْرِمُونَ (۸)
so that He may confirm the truth and bring falsehood to naught, though the guilty should be averse. (8)

Key Points

This Ayah gives solace to the Prophet of Islam صلى الله علیه وآله and the Muslims that ultimately the truth (Haqq) will be victorious and falsehood (Batil) will be annihilated. Some narrations consider the clear manifestation (Miṣdāq) of this Ayah to be the time of the advent (Ẓuhūr) of Hadrat Mahdi علیه السلام, [429] when, through his uprising, the dominance of falsehood will be removed and the government of truth and justice will rule the world.

429) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn

Messages

1- Divine promises are not for the sake of personal or material interests of individuals, but rather for the realization of truth and the eradication of falsehood. «لِيُحِقَّ الْحَقَّ»

2- Truth (Haqq) is lasting and enduring, while falsehood (Batil) is perishable and transient. «لِيُحِقَّ الْحَقَّ وَيُبْطِلَ الْبَاطِلَ»

3- We should not fear the anger or displeasure of the disbelieving enemy and the criminals; Allah will certainly realize His will. «وَلَوْ كَرِهَ الْمُجْرِمُونَ»

Ayah

إِذْ تَسْتَغِيثُونَ رَبَّكُمْ فَٱسْتَجَابَ لَكُمْ أَنِّى مُمِدُّكُم بِأَلْفٍ مِّنَ ٱلْمَلَـٰٓئِكَةِ مُرْدِفِينَ (۹)
When you appealed to your Lord for help, He answered you: ‘I will aid you with a thousand angels in a file.’ (9)

Key Points

Murdaf (مُردف) comes from Irdaf (اِرداف), meaning to be arranged in a row or one after the other. This means the descent of the assisting angels is continuous and successive. In Ayah 124 of Surah Aal-i-Imran, three thousand assisting angels are mentioned, "ثلاثةآلاف" (three thousand), and in the Ayah following it, there is talk of five thousand marked angels. This difference in numbers might relate to the level of their steadfastness. Meaning, the greater the resistance of the Muslims, the greater Allah's unseen aid became. Therefore, murdafin (مردفین) means that these thousand angels are followed by other angels. Considering the next Ayah, the angels descended only to reassure and strengthen the morale of the believers, and they did not enter into battle or combat with the enemy. Otherwise, there would have been no merit left for the Mujahideen of Badr. Furthermore, the manner in which each of the disbelievers was killed and the names of their killers are recorded in history. [430]

In the Battle of Badr, the number of Muslims was one-third that of the disbelievers, and their military equipment was also very scarce. According to the statement of Hadrat Ali, عليه السلام, their only horseman was Miqdad. [431] Furthermore, they lacked the psychological readiness for war. When they saw the multitude of disbelievers and their equipment, they became terrified and anxious and sought refuge in Allah. The Noble Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله also raised his hands in supplication and said: "الّلهم انجز لى ما وعدتنى، الّلهم ان تهلك هذه العصابة لا تُعبَد فى الارض" [432] O God! Fulfill what You have promised me. O God! If this group of Muslims is destroyed, Your worship will cease on earth. Allah, too, answered their prayer by pouring down His unseen aids, and they fought with reassurance until they finally achieved victory. By the order of the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله, all the slain disbelievers were thrown into a well. Then the Prophet came to the edge of the well, called them out one by one by name, and said: Did you find the promise of your Lord true?! You were evil people, for you denied your Prophet, drove me out of my home and dwelling, and fought against me. But others confirmed me, gave me shelter among them, and assisted me! The companions asked: Do they hear? The Prophet replied: You are not more capable of hearing than they are, but they cannot reply. Then He turned towards the corpse of Abu Jahl and said: This one was more insolent than Pharaoh, because Pharaoh became a monotheist when the punishment came and he saw destruction was certain, but this one did not abandon the idols until the very last moments. [433]

430) Tafsir Namuneh 431) Tafsir Furqan 432) Tafsir Kanz al-Daqaiq 433) Tafsirs Al-Mizan, Namuneh, and Furqan

Messages

  1. Let us not forget Allah's blessings and aids during difficult days, because remembering divine blessings raises the spirit of gratitude and courage in a person. "اذ" (when)

  2. Allah can grant without supplication, but prayer is a path of divine training. "اذ تستغیثون ربّكم" (when you sought help from your Lord)

  3. Supplication is effective alongside presence on the battlefield, and the prayers of the combatants are answered. "تستغیثون... فاستجاب" (you sought help... so He answered)

  4. Supplication is the key to acceptance (of prayer). "تستغیثون... فاستجاب" (you sought help... so He answered)

  5. Angels are influential and effective in human lives. "ممدّكم بألف من الملائكة" (He reinforced you with a thousand angels)

  6. Unseen aids materialize when we have utilized what Allah has given us outwardly. "ممدّكم" (He reinforced you)

  7. In military movements, operations, and battle scenes, necessary order and discipline are factors that pave the way for victory. "انّى ممدّكم بألف من الملائكة مردفین" (I am reinforcing you with a thousand angels, following one another)

Ayah

وَمَا جَعَلَهُ ٱللَّهُ إِلَّا بُشْرَىٰ وَلِتَطْمَئِنَّ بِهِۦ قُلُوبُكُمْ ۚ وَمَا ٱلنَّصْرُ إِلَّا مِنْ عِندِ ٱللَّهِ ۚ إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ عَزِيزٌ حَكِيمٌ (۱٠)
Allah did not appoint it but as a good news, and to reassure your hearts. Victory comes only from Allah. Indeed Allah is all-mighty, all-wise. (10)

Key Points

The descent of assisting angels to aid the believers has been mentioned repeatedly in the Quran. Even at the moment of death, Allah sends angels to protect the believer from the whispers of Satan by inspiring truth and consolation. [434] In the Battle of Badr, too, the angels came to boost the morale of the believers and did not engage in fighting or confrontation with the enemy, because history clearly shows who was killed by whom, and the majority of the casualties among the disbelievers in that battle were by the sword of Hadrat Ali عليه السلام. There are two types of inspiration (or suggestion) in existence: one is from Allah's angels, who inspire tranquility. «اذ یوحى ربّك الى الملائكة أنّى معكم فثبّتوا الّذین آمنوا سألقى فى قلوب الّذین كفروا الرُّعب» [435] Allah revealed to the angels, "I am with you, so make the believers steadfast and firm. I will cast terror into the hearts of the disbelievers." The other is the inspiration of fear and terror, which is carried out by Satan. «انّما ذلكم الشیطان یخوّف أولیائه» [436] Satan's words influence his followers and frighten them.

434) Fussilat, 30 435) Anfal, 12 436) Aal-i-Imran, 175

Messages

1- A combatant must possess strong morale. «وماجعله اللّه الاّ بُشرى» Strong motivation and morale among the fighters are factors for victory.

2- When an action is correct and aligned with the path of the Prophet, Allah's favor and the removal of factors causing fear and anxiety are certain. «وما جعله اللّه الاّ بُشرى»

3- The victory of the believers is not solely due to their initiative, military planning, weapons, personnel, or even the angels; rather, the fundamental factor is the will and desire of Allah. «وما النّصر الاّ من عند اللّه» Just as sometimes, by the will of Allah, a small group of believers overcomes a large group of enemies. «كم من فئة قلیلة غلبت فئة كثیرة باذن اللّه» [437]

4- Divine assistance and the aid given to believers are based on might and wisdom. «عزیز حكیم»

437) Baqarah, 249

Ayah

إِذْ يُغَشِّيكُمُ ٱلنُّعَاسَ أَمَنَةً مِّنْهُ وَيُنَزِّلُ عَلَيْكُم مِّنَ ٱلسَّمَآءِ مَآءً لِّيُطَهِّرَكُم بِهِۦ وَيُذْهِبَ عَنكُمْ رِجْزَ ٱلشَّيْطَـٰنِ وَلِيَرْبِطَ عَلَىٰ قُلُوبِكُمْ وَيُثَبِّتَ بِهِ ٱلْأَقْدَامَ (۱۱)
When He covered you with a trance as a [sense of] security from Him, and He sent down water from the sky to purify you with it, and to repel from you the defilement of Satan, and to fortify your hearts, and to make [your] feet steady with it. (11)

Key Points

Nu'as (slumber/drowsiness) refers to the beginning of sleep, light, and soothing sleep. This means that during your rest, such a deep sleep did not overcome you that the enemy could exploit it and launch a night attack against you. [438] The Quraysh army entered the region of Badr with abundant military equipment, sufficient provisions, and even singing women to boost the morale of the fighters. They initially took control of the water wells, but the Muslims were unsettled. The Prophet, seeing that His companions might not sleep peacefully that night and would face the enemy the next day with tired bodies and spirits, gave them the glad tidings that divine angels would come to their aid. He comforted them so much that they slept peacefully through the night. On the other hand, besides the shortage of water for purification (Tahara) and quenching thirst, the fundamental problem in the Badr region was the presence of soft, shifting sands in which feet would sink. That night, rain fell, and the Muslims were encouraged, and the ground beneath their feet became firm. Of course, the intent of thabaat al-qadam (firmness of foot) in the Ayah might be steadfastness and perseverance, rather than the hardening of the ground beneath their feet due to the rain. Both possibilities are also plausible simultaneously. The intent of "being purified from filth" might be purification from the whispers of Satan, or purification from physical impurity due to janabah (major ritual impurity) experienced by some that night. In any case, the rain, given the severe need of the Muslims, resolved all these problems. [439] Imam Sadiq عليه السلام said: Drink rainwater, for it removes illnesses. Then He recited this Ayah: [440] «یذهب عنكم رجز الشیطان»

438) Tafsir-e Nemooneh 439) Tafsir-e Nemooneh 440) Kafi, Vol. 6, p. 387

Messages

  1. With divine will and aid, one can have peace and sleep soundly even when facing a heavily armed and numerous enemy. If Allah does not will it, there is neither sleep nor peace, even in the best gardens and conditions. «اذ یغشّیكم النعاس أمنة منه»

  2. Sometimes, rest and light sleep during war are great divine blessings, as they both remove fatigue and prevent the enemy from launching a night attack. «النّعاس أمنة منه»

  3. If there is faith and patience, Allah places natural factors at the disposal of man and for his benefit. «ینزّل علیكم من السماء ماءً»

  4. The role of natural factors like wind, rain, sleep, etc., in the battlefields should not be considered accidental. «یغشّیكم النعاس... ینزّل علیكم...»

  5. Outer purity: «لیطهّركم» (that He might purify you) and inner purity: «یذهب عنكم رجز الشیطان» (that He might remove from you the filth of Satan), each is valuable on its own, but the existence of both together is more valuable.

  6. Allah's expectation from the Muslim warrior is purity, high and resilient morale, and steadfastness. «لیطهرّكم... لیربط على قلوبكم»

Ayah

إِذْ يُوحِى رَبُّكَ إِلَى ٱلْمَلَـٰٓئِكَةِ أَنِّى مَعَكُمْ فَثَبِّتُوا۟ ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ ۚ سَأُلْقِى فِى قُلُوبِ ٱلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا۟ ٱلرُّعْبَ فَٱضْرِبُوا۟ فَوْقَ ٱلْأَعْنَاقِ وَٱضْرِبُوا۟ مِنْهُمْ كُلَّ بَنَانٍ (۱۲)
Then your Lord signaled to the angels: ‘I am indeed with you; so steady the faithful. I will cast terror into the hearts of the faithless. So strike their necks, and strike their every limb joint!’ (12)

Key Points

«بَنان» (Banan) is the plural of «bananah», meaning the fingertip or toe tip, or the fingers and toes. Striking the head and brain of the disbelievers, or hitting their fingertips and toes, removes their ability to fight and hold weapons. It is also possible that this indicates that if the enemy is on foot, the target should be their head, and if they are mounted, target their hands and feet. [441] Perhaps the intent of «fawqa al-a'naq» (above the necks) refers to prominent individuals and leaders of disbelief, similar to: «Fa qātilū a'immat al-kufr» (So fight the leaders of disbelief), meaning the strike should be delivered to the heads and leaders of the enemy. [442] Among the Divine aids in the Battle of Badr was the creation of dread and terror in the hearts of the armies of disbelief, just as the secret reporters of the army of Islam brought news from the enemy camp that the enemy, despite all their resources and equipment, was extremely frightened and apprehensive, but in contrast, the small army of Islam, despite the scarcity of personnel and resources, had very high morale. Therefore, the Prophet initially offered them peace and sent a representative to them, although some individuals agreed with this proposal, Abu Jahl rejected it, and the fire of war was ignited.

441) Tafsir Namūneh 442) Tafsir Furqan

Messages

1- Allah sometimes guides and supports the believers through the angels. «Idh yūḥī Rabbuka ilā al-malā'ikati...»

2- Angels have no power of their own; rather, they gain power through Divine support and grace. «Idh yūḥī Rabbuka ilā al-malā'ikati innī ma‘akum fa thabbitū»

3- Allah assigned the encouragement for steadfastness through the angels, but attributed the intimidation of the disbelievers to Himself. «Fa thabbitū, sa'ulqī»

4- Allah sends down tranquility and peace upon the people of faith, and causes anxiety and terror to overcome the disbelievers. «Idh yūḥī Rabbuka... fa thabbitū alladhīna āmanū sa'ulqī fī qulūbi alladhīna kafarū al-ru‘b»

5- Hearts are in the hand of Allah; tranquility and anxiety also originate from Him. «Idh yūḥī Rabbuka.. fa thabbitū... sa'ulqī... al-ru‘b»

6- Dread and terror were among the factors leading to the defeat of the disbelievers in the Battle of Badr. «Sa'ulqī fī qulūbi alladhīna kafarū al-ru‘b»

7- Human inclinations and motivations have a significant impact on their destiny and on receiving Divine mercy or punishment. «Fa thabbitū alladhīna āmanū... fī qulūbi alladhīna kafarū al-ru‘b»

8- Power and resources alone are not the cause of tranquility. (In the Battle of Badr, Muslims found peace despite few numbers, while the numerous and well-equipped enemies became terrified.) «Amanatan minhu - al-ru‘b»

9- Make the best use of your combat ability in battle and deliver strikes to sensitive areas. «Faḍribū fawqa al-a‘nāqi waḍribū minhum kulla banān»

Ayahs

ذَٰلِكَ بِأَنَّهُمْ شَآقُّوا۟ ٱللَّهَ وَرَسُولَهُۥ ۚ وَمَن يُشَاقِقِ ٱللَّهَ وَرَسُولَهُۥ فَإِنَّ ٱللَّهَ شَدِيدُ ٱلْعِقَابِ (۱۳)
That, because they defied Allah and His Apostle. And whoever defies Allah and His Apostle, Allah is indeed severe in retribution. (13)
ذَٰلِكُمْ فَذُوقُوهُ وَأَنَّ لِلْكَـٰفِرِينَ عَذَابَ ٱلنَّارِ (۱٤)
‘Taste this, and [know] that for the faithless is the punishment of the Fire.’ (14)

Messages

1- Divine wrath and anger are the result of rebellion and transgression; they are not without reason or calculation. «ذلك بأنّهم...»

2- Opposition to the Messenger of God is opposition to Allah. «شاقّوا اللّه و رسوله»

3- It is the way of Allah that whoever opposes the truth will be destroyed. «و مَن یشاقق... فانّ اللّه شدید العقاب»

4- The disbelievers are afflicted by vengeance and destruction both in this world and by punishment in the Hereafter. «فاضربوا فوق الاعناق - للكافرین عذاب النّار»

5- Worldly punishments, however severe they may be, are only to the extent of tasting the punishments of the Hereafter. «ذلكم فذوقوه و انّ للكافرین عذاب النّار»

Ayah

يَـٰٓأَيُّهَا ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوٓا۟ إِذَا لَقِيتُمُ ٱلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا۟ زَحْفًا فَلَا تُوَلُّوهُمُ ٱلْأَدْبَارَ (۱٥)
O you who have faith! When you encounter the faithless advancing [for battle], do not turn your backs [to flee] from them. (15)

Key Points

'Zahf' (زَحف) means crawling or dragging oneself on the ground. The movement of a massive army is also called zahf because, from a distance, it appears as if they are crawling and advancing on the ground. Imam Reza عليه السلام said: Fleeing from Jihad causes weakness in religion, disrespect toward the rightful leader, emboldening of the enemy, and the obliteration of the faith. (443) Among the virtues of Hadrat Ali عليه السلام, it is mentioned that throughout his life, he never fled from the battlefield or war, not even once. (444) And that Imam said: The most desirable death is martyrdom. I swear by Him in Whose hand is the soul of the son of Abu Talib, being killed by a thousand sword strikes is easier and more comfortable for me than dying in bed. (445)

(443) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn (444) Tafasir Namuneh and Nur al-Thaqalayn (445) Nahj al-Balaghah, Sermon 121

Messages

1- Allah has special expectations of the believers. «یا ایّها الّذین آمنوا...»

2- The large number of the enemy does not justify fleeing the battlefield. «اذا لقیتم... زحفاً فلا تولّوهم»

3- During combat, fleeing the war is not permissible. «اذا لقیتم... فلا تولّوهم»

(However, fleeing is prohibited when two groups meet each other prepared for confrontation. But if the armed enemy launches a surprise attack and the Muslims are unprepared, retreat is permissible for those who are unarmed. (446))

4- Islamic wars are ideological, not colonial, based on whims, desires, or similar motives. «الّذین آمنوا... الّذین كفروا»

(446) Tafsir Furqan

Ayah

وَمَن يُوَلِّهِمْ يَوْمَئِذٍ دُبُرَهُۥٓ إِلَّا مُتَحَرِّفًا لِّقِتَالٍ أَوْ مُتَحَيِّزًا إِلَىٰ فِئَةٍ فَقَدْ بَآءَ بِغَضَبٍ مِّنَ ٱللَّهِ وَمَأْوَىٰهُ جَهَنَّمُ ۖ وَبِئْسَ ٱلْمَصِيرُ (۱٦)
Whoever turns his back [to flee] from them that day—unless [he is] diverting to fight or retiring towards another troop—shall certainly earn Allah’s wrath, and his refuge shall be hell, an evil destination. (16)

Key Points

"مُتحرّفاً" (mutaharrifan) means withdrawing oneself to the side or periphery to change tactics, exhaust and mislead the enemy, and then strike him. And "متحیّزاً" (mutahayyizan) means joining and taking a place alongside another group when the fighter feels isolated and unable to cope. The phrase "باء بغضب" (bā'a bi-ghaḍab) means to incur or bear the wrath (of Allah). In this Ayah, two exceptions are mentioned for fleeing the battlefield, which is otherwise forbidden (haram): one is for tactical repositioning ("متحرّفاً"), and the other is for joining another group of Muslims for a collective attack ("مُتحیّزاً"). Of course, some commentaries mention other instances, such as fleeing to inform the Muslims, or to preserve a more important position or better strategic location. (447) However, these are all examples falling under the first case. Those who flee the battlefield incur the anger and wrath of Allah: "و مَن یولّهم... فقد باء بغضب من اللّه" (And whoever turns his back to them... has certainly incurred wrath from Allah). They are among those from whom we seek disassociation in every prayer: "غیر المغضوب علیهم" (not those upon whom is wrath). However, this sin is forgivable through repentance, based on the Ayah: "انّ الّذین تولّوا منكم یوم التقى الجَمعان انّما استزلّهم الشیطان ببعض ما كسبوا و لقد عفا اللّه عنهم" (Indeed, those of you who turned back on the day the two armies met—it was Satan who caused them to slip because of some [evil] they had earned. But Allah has certainly forgiven them). (448)

447) Tafsir Fi Zilal al-Qur'an 448) Al Imran, 155

Messages

1- Fleeing the battlefield is a major sin (Ghunah Kabirah), and Allah has promised wrath and punishment for it. "و من یولّهم... فقد باء بغضب من اللّه"

2- Tactical retreat is permissible. "متحرّفاً"

3- Deception and misleading the enemy are permissible in war. "متحرّفاً"

4- Just as going to the battlefield is important, so too is not fleeing. Sometimes people go to the front line, but by fleeing, they become inhabitants of Hell. "و من یولّهم یومئذ... مأواه جهنّم"

5- We should not judge hastily in evaluations, because a change in action might be due to a plan or tactic. "متحرّفاً"

6- Victory (Nuṣrah) comes from Allah, but employing military techniques, tactics, and war strategy is also necessary. "متحرّفاً لقتال أو متحیّزاً"

7- The abode of those who flee Jihad and those who incur Divine wrath is Hell. "مأواه جهنّم" (In reality, whoever flees from Jihad seeks refuge and shelter, but finds no shelter except the fire of Hell.)

8- Fleeing from war brings both worldly disgrace ("باء بغضب") and punishment in the Hereafter ("مأواه جهنّم"). Those who flee face an evil destiny. "بئس المصیر"

Ayah

فَلَمْ تَقْتُلُوهُمْ وَلَـٰكِنَّ ٱللَّهَ قَتَلَهُمْ ۚ وَمَا رَمَيْتَ إِذْ رَمَيْتَ وَلَـٰكِنَّ ٱللَّهَ رَمَىٰ ۚ وَلِيُبْلِىَ ٱلْمُؤْمِنِينَ مِنْهُ بَلَآءً حَسَنًا ۚ إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ سَمِيعٌ عَلِيمٌ (۱۷)
You did not kill them; rather, it was Allah who killed them; and you did not throw when you threw, rather, it was Allah who threw, that He might test the faithful with a good test from Himself. Indeed Allah is all-hearing, all-knowing. (17)

Key Points

During the Battle of Badr, the Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله told Ali عليه السلام: "Pick up a handful of dust and pebbles from the ground and give them to Me." Ali عليه السلام did so, and the Prophet, after reciting a supplication, threw them towards the enemy. This act had a miraculous effect; it raised a storm and caused a strange terror in the hearts of the enemy. [449] Yes, the crucial factor for victory in the Battle of Badr was the Divine Will and assistance, not material strength, military rules, or tactics. Otherwise, how could a small army, with only one or two mounted horses, defeat a massive, fully equipped army possessing one hundred mounted horses?

Balā’ (بلاء) means trial or test. If it involves blessings and victory, it is balā’ ḥasan (بلاء حَسَن - a good trial). If it involves calamity and punishment, it is balā’ sayyi’ (بلاء سیّى ء - a bad trial), just as the Quran says regarding the Children of Israel: «و بَلَوناهم بِالحَسنات و السیّئات» [450]

If a person moves with correct belief along the path of truth and strives according to the Divine command, he will receive the assistance of Allah, «لیبلى المؤمنین منه بلاء حسناً» (so that He might test the believers with a good trial from Him). His existence becomes a Divine existence, just as Allah says in a narration: The servant reaches the station of proximity through nāfilah (supererogatory acts) and recommended deeds, and his hand becomes My Hand, his ear My Ear, and all his limbs and organs become Divine, and whatever he desires comes to pass. «انّه لیتقرّب الىّ بالنافلة حتّى اُحبّه فاذا احببتُه كنتُ سمعه الّذى یسمع به و بصره الّذى یبصر به و لسانه الّذى ینطق به و یده الّذى یبطش بها اِن دعانى أجبتُه و اِن سألنى أعطیتُه...» [451] Therefore, if he performs an action, that action is, in reality, from Allah. «و ما رمیتَ اذ رمیت و لكنّ اللّه رَمى»

Conversely, if he moves along a non-Divine path and under the command of others, he has, in reality, acquired a Satanic existence, just as Aisha, during the confrontation with the army of Amir al-Mu’minin Ali عليه السلام in the Battle of the Camel, threw a handful of dust towards them and supplicated, imitating the Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله. Afterwards, Hadrat Ali عليه السلام told her: "It was Satan who did this." «و ما رمیتِ اذ رمیتِ و لكنّ الشیطان رمى». [452]

449) Tafasir Al-Mizan and Namuneh 450) Al-A'raf, 168, Tafsir Namuneh 451) Kafi, Vol. 2, p. 352 452) Sharh Nahj al-Balaghah by Ibn Abi al-Hadid, Vol. 1, p. 257

Messages

1- Do not let victory in war lead to arrogance or vanity; do not forget Allah and His assistance. «فلم تقتلوهم و لكنّ اللّه قَتَلهم...»

2- Man is neither independent of Allah’s Will nor compelled in his actions. Actions are attributed to man because they originate from him by choice, but since the power and effect come from Allah, they are attributed to Him. «مارمیتَ... ولكنّ اللّه رَمى»

3- The presence of the Prophet and the leader plays a significant role in attracting Allah’s unseen assistance. «وما رمیتَ اذ رمیتَ...»

4- War and Jihad are among the means of Divine testing, so that true believers may be distinguished from those weak in faith or faithless. «بلاء حسناً»

5- Divine assistance, the victory of the believers, and the defeat of the enemies are not accidental or random; rather, they are based on Allah’s knowledge and awareness. «اِنّ اللّه سمیع علیم»

Ayah

ذَٰلِكُمْ وَأَنَّ ٱللَّهَ مُوهِنُ كَيْدِ ٱلْكَـٰفِرِينَ (۱۸)
Such is the case, and [know] that Allah undermines the stratagems of the faithless. (18)

Key Points

«ذلكم» refers to the situation of the Muslims and the disbelievers in the Battle of Badr, and reminds [us] of the divine aids from the heaven and the earth and the defeat of the enemy. It is as if He said: «ذلكم لطف اللّه علیكم» The weakening of the enemies' plots was achieved by creating dread and fear in their hearts, [causing] instability and division, the exposure of secrets, storms, and thunder and lightning.

Messages

1- Allah neutralizes the plots of the enemies against the believers. «انّ اللّه موهن كید الكافرین» (If the spirit of faith is maintained, Allah neutralizes and shatters even the hidden and imperceptible matters of the enemies against us, just as the Tabas incident occurred in our time, while we were asleep or did not even imagine it.)

2- Faith is the condition for receiving divine support, and disbelief is the ground for human weakness and destruction. «ذلكم و انّ اللّه موهن كید الكافرین»

3- In all circumstances, we must rely upon Allah and have no fear of the enemies and their conspiracies. «انّ اللّه موهن كید الكافرین»

Ayah

إِن تَسْتَفْتِحُوا۟ فَقَدْ جَآءَكُمُ ٱلْفَتْحُ ۖ وَإِن تَنتَهُوا۟ فَهُوَ خَيْرٌ لَّكُمْ ۖ وَإِن تَعُودُوا۟ نَعُدْ وَلَن تُغْنِىَ عَنكُمْ فِئَتُكُمْ شَيْـًٔا وَلَوْ كَثُرَتْ وَأَنَّ ٱللَّهَ مَعَ ٱلْمُؤْمِنِينَ (۱۹)
If you sought a verdict, the verdict has certainly come to you; and if you cease [your belligerence against the Prophet and his followers], it is better for you, but if you resume, We [too] shall return and your troops will never avail you though they should be ever so many, and [know] that Allah is with the faithful. (19)

Key Points

Regarding who the addressee of this Ayah is, two possibilities have been stated: one, the disbelievers defeated in the Battle of Badr, and the other, the Muslims who disagreed over the division of the spoils of war. Supporting the first possibility is the connection of this Ayah with the preceding Ayahs, "انّ اللّه موهن كید الكافرین" (Indeed, Allah is the frustrator of the plot of the disbelievers), just as they were the large group ("فئة كثیرة") and they were the ones who had a history of disbelief and incurring wrath. And in this Ayah, He says: "و ان تعودوا نعد... و لو كثرت" (And if you return, We will return... even if it is numerous). Also, the statement of Abu Jahl, the commander of the polytheists, who, upon leaving Mecca for the battle, grabbed the curtain of the Ka'bah and said: O God! Grant victory to the group that is more guided among these two factions. [453] He was confident of his victory, but ultimately he was defeated. However, if the second possibility is adopted, and the address is directed at the Muslims, it is a warning to them that after this victory, they should cease their disputes and objections. If they continue their previous grumbling and arguments (objections before the battle and during the division of the spoils), Allah's favor will again be withheld from them, and they will be abandoned against their enemies and suffer defeat. (Of course, the first meaning seems more appropriate.)

453) Tafsir Majma' al-Bayan

Messages

1- The good and welfare of man lies in avoiding deviation and confrontation with Allah and His Messenger. "ان تنتهوا فهو خیر لكم"

2- Receiving Allah's favor or His wrath depends on our own choice and action. "ان تنتهوا... ان تعودوا"

3- Allah has completed the argument (made the truth clear) and closed all avenues for excuse-making and pretexts. "ان تنتهوا... ان تعودوا"

4- Encouragement and warning are both necessary side by side. "خیرلكم و ان تعودوا نعد"

5- Wrongdoings must be stopped with decisiveness and clarity. "ان تعودوا نعد" Forgiveness and pardon are not always desirable.

6- Large numbers have no role or effect against Allah's wrath. "لن تغنى عنكم فئتكم شیئاً و لو كثرت"

7- Faith is the condition for Allah's help and companionship. "انّ اللّه مع المؤمنین"

Ayahs

يَـٰٓأَيُّهَا ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوٓا۟ أَطِيعُوا۟ ٱللَّهَ وَرَسُولَهُۥ وَلَا تَوَلَّوْا۟ عَنْهُ وَأَنتُمْ تَسْمَعُونَ (۲٠)
O you who have faith! Obey Allah and His Apostle, and do not turn away from him while you hear [him]. (20)
وَلَا تَكُونُوا۟ كَٱلَّذِينَ قَالُوا۟ سَمِعْنَا وَهُمْ لَا يَسْمَعُونَ (۲۱)
Do not be like those who say, ‘We hear,’ though they do not hear. (21)

Key Points

Throughout the Quran, obedience to the Messenger of Allah is mentioned immediately after obedience to Allah. In eleven instances, the phrase "أطیعون" (obey Me) follows the expression "اتّقوا اللّه" (Fear Allah), meaning that the prerequisite for piety (Taqwa) toward Allah is obedience to His Messenger. Although obedience to both Allah and the Messenger is necessary, in this Ayah, only turning away from the Messenger is prohibited, which indicates that their primary difficulty lay in obeying the Messenger, especially during the Battle of Badr and regarding the Prophet's صلى الله علیه وآله commands concerning military matters. The intent behind hearing the words of the Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله, "و انتم تسمعون" (while you are hearing), based on the following Ayah, is understanding His words and realizing His truthfulness.

Messages

1- Believers are susceptible to violating the commands of Allah and the Messenger and require warnings. "یا ایّها الّذین آمنوا أطیعوا..." (O you who have believed, obey...)

2- Disobedience to the Prophet is disobedience to Allah. "و لا تولّوا عنه" (and do not turn away from him), and He did not say, "عنهما" (from both of them). (Indeed, the command of Allah and the Messenger possess a single identity, and obedience to Allah is contingent upon obedience to His Messenger.)

3- Hearing and understanding bring responsibility. "و لا تولَّوا عنه و انتم تسمعون" (and do not turn away from him while you are hearing).

4- Becoming aware of the history and accounts of past transgressors serves as a lesson. "ولاتكونوا كالّذین..." (And do not be like those who...)

5- In obeying leadership, be sincere, adhere to your commitments, and do not claim faith without action. "و لا تكونوا كالّذین قالوا سمعنا و هم لایسمعون" (And do not be like those who say, 'We have heard,' but they do not hear).

Ayah

۞ إِنَّ شَرَّ ٱلدَّوَآبِّ عِندَ ٱللَّهِ ٱلصُّمُّ ٱلْبُكْمُ ٱلَّذِينَ لَا يَعْقِلُونَ (۲۲)
Indeed the worst of beasts in Allah’s sight are the deaf and dumb who do not exercise their reason. (22)

Key Points

Ṣumm (صُمّ) is the plural of aṣamm (أصَمّ), meaning deaf, and bukm (بُكم) is the plural of abkam (أبكَم), meaning mute. Here, deaf and mute are metaphors for those who do not lend an ear to hearing the truth and who seal their lips from speaking the truth. In Quranic expressions, individuals who do not accept the divine training of the Prophets and do not surrender their hearts to the truth are sometimes likened to the dead: «انّك لا تسمع الموتى» [454]; sometimes to cattle: «اولئك كالانعام» [455], «یأكلون كما تأكل الانعام» [456]; sometimes to worse than cattle: «كالانعام بل هم أضلّ» [457]; and sometimes to the worst of creatures: «شرّ الدّوابّ». Imam Ali عليه السلام said: The worst of creatures are those who understand the truth but persist in obstinacy and commit disbelief (kufr). [458] A group will confess on the Day of Judgment that they did not listen to the word of truth or did not reason about it, and this was the cause of their consignment to Hellfire. «لو كنّا نسمع أو نعقل ما كنّا فى اصحاب السعیر» [459] If we had listened or reasoned, we would not have been among the inhabitants of the Blaze.

Messages

1- Those who claim faith but are not committed to obeying Allah and the Messenger—«و لا تكونوا كالّذین قالوا سمعنا و هم لایسمعون» (and be not like those who say, "We have heard," while they do not hear)—are the worst of creatures. «انّ شرّ الدّوابّ»

2- The value of a human being lies in his wisdom and reasoning. If he does not reason, he becomes the worst of creatures. «انّ شرّ الدّواب... الّذین لایعقلون» (Indeed, having ears, a tongue, and intellect is only valuable when they are used correctly. For example, a person who does not enjoin good and forbid evil with his tongue is like a mute person.)

3- Those who turn away from the teachings of religion are senseless. «و لا تولّوا عنه... لا یعقلون»

Ayah

وَلَوْ عَلِمَ ٱللَّهُ فِيهِمْ خَيْرًا لَّأَسْمَعَهُمْ ۖ وَلَوْ أَسْمَعَهُمْ لَتَوَلَّوا۟ وَّهُم مُّعْرِضُونَ (۲۳)
Had Allah known any good in them, He would have surely made them hear, and were He to make them hear, surely they would turn away, being disregardful. (23)

Key Points

Stubborn individuals fall into several groups: Some are not even willing to hear the truth. «لا تسمعوا لهذا القرآن» [460] Some hear, but mock and ridicule. «قالوا قد سمعنا لو نشاء لقلنا مثل هذا» [461] Some hear and understand, but distort (the words). «یحرّفون الكلم عن مواضعه و یقولون سمعنا و عصینا» [462] Some, due to intense attachments, jealousies, and hardness of heart, lack the power of discernment. «و جعلنا قلوبهم قاسیة» [463]

460) Fussilat, 26 461) Anfal, 31 462) Nisa, 46 463) Ma'idah, 13

Messages

1- It is among the Divine traditions that He grants His grace (faydh) to receptive individuals, and He grants success and favor to everyone according to the merit and capacity for accepting the truth that they have cultivated within themselves. «و لو علم اللّه فیهم خیراً لاسمعهم»

2- We must ourselves create the conditions for benefiting from Divine favor. «و لو علم اللّه فیهم خیراً لاسمعهم»

3- Merely learning and hearing the verses is not the criterion; accepting the truth and submission are also necessary. «و لو أسمعهم لتولّوا»

4- Allah does not withhold guidance from people, but the stubborn turn away from the word of truth. «لو علم... لاسمعهم»

5- Man possesses free will and can turn away in the face of the call to truth. «و لو اسمعهم لتولّوا و هم معرضون» (Indeed, whoever blinds his own potential for growth will not accept the grace of Allah either.)

Ayah

يَـٰٓأَيُّهَا ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ ٱسْتَجِيبُوا۟ لِلَّهِ وَلِلرَّسُولِ إِذَا دَعَاكُمْ لِمَا يُحْيِيكُمْ ۖ وَٱعْلَمُوٓا۟ أَنَّ ٱللَّهَ يَحُولُ بَيْنَ ٱلْمَرْءِ وَقَلْبِهِۦ وَأَنَّهُۥٓ إِلَيْهِ تُحْشَرُونَ (۲٤)
O you who have faith! Answer Allah and the Apostle when he summons you to that which will give you life. Know that Allah intervenes between a man and his heart and that toward Him you will be mustered. (24)

Key Points

Life has different types: 1- Vegetative life. «انّ اللّه یحیى الارض بعد موتها» [464] Allah revives the earth after its death. 2- Animal life. «لمحیى الموتى» [465] Allah revives the dead. 3- Intellectual life. «مَن كان میّتاً فأحییناه» [466] He who was dead, and We revived him. 4- Eternal life. «قدّمت لحیاتى» [467] I wish I had sent forth [good deeds] for my life. The life intended that is achieved through the invitation of the Prophets is not animal life, because this type of life exists even without the Prophets' invitation. Rather, the intended life is intellectual, rational, spiritual, moral, and social life—in short, life in all fields. Of course, it is possible that the meaning of the invitation to life here (due to the Ayah being placed alongside the verses about the Battle of Badr) is an invitation to Jihad (struggle). Human life lies in faith and righteous deeds, and Allah and the Prophets have invited people to exactly that. «دعاكم لما یحییكم» Obedience to their commands is the key to achieving a pure and good life (Hayat Tayyibah), just as we read elsewhere: «مَن عمل صالحاً من ذكر أو أنثى و هو مؤمن فلنحیینّه حیاة طیّبة» [468] And according to Shi'a and Sunni narrations, one of the manifestations of the good life (Hayat Tayyibah) is accepting the call of the Prophet of Islam صلى الله علیه وآله regarding the matter of the Wilayah (guardianship) of Ali ibn Abi Talib علیهما السلام and his Household. [469]

Allah intervening and coming between a person and his heart indicates Allah’s presence and observation everywhere, His encompassing control over all beings, and that power and success (divine grace) come from Him. He is closer to us than the jugular vein; the activity of the intellect and the soul is also in His hand, and were it not for His grace, man would never realize the truthfulness of the truth and the falsehood of the false. It is narrated from Imam Sadiq عليه السلام that one of the manifestations of Allah intervening between a person and his heart is the issue of effacement and affirmation (Mahw wa Ithbat): the effacement of disbelief and the affirmation of faith, the effacement of heedlessness and doubt, and the establishment of remembrance and certainty. [470] In another place, He (the Imam) said: Allah does not allow a person to see falsehood as truth. [471] He also said: Sometimes a person desires something with his ear, eye, tongue, and hand, but if he performs it, his heart does not accompany it, and he knows that what he desired was not the truth. [472] And it is possible that the intervention is a metaphor for death.

Messages

1- The condition for true faith is responding to the invitation of Allah and the Messenger. «یا ایها الّذین آمنوا استجیبوا للّه و للرّسول»

2- The invitation of Allah and the Messenger possesses a single reality and identity. «دعاكم» (He invited you) and He did not say: «دعواكم» (They invited you).

3- Movement in the path of Allah and the Prophets is true life, and withdrawal from it is the death of humanity. «دعاكم لما یحییكم»

4- All religious commands and the orders of the Prophets, even the command for war and Jihad, are the source of life for the individual and society. «دعاكم لما یحییكم»

5- Whoever believes in the presence and encompassing control of Allah will not disobey the invitation of the Prophets. «استجیبوا... یحول بین المرء و قلبه»

6- Let us accept the truth before we leave this world and while we have the opportunity. (Based on the interpretation that Allah intervening between a person and his heart is a metaphor for death). «استجیبوا... یحول بین المرء و قلبه»

7- Let not the believer become arrogant, nor should one despair of the disbeliever, because hearts are in the hand of Allah, and He is the Changer of Hearts (Muqallib al-Qulub). «یحول بین المرء و قلبه»

8- There is no excuse for rejecting the Tawhid (Oneness) and unity of Allah. «یحول بین المرء و قلبه» because Tawhid is an innate matter (a matter of Fitrah). [473]

9- Allah is aware of every thought and idea that passes through a person's heart. «یحول بین المرء و قلبه»

10- Allah is closer to man than anything, even closer than his own heart to himself. «یحول بین المرء و قلبه»

11- All of us, willingly or unwillingly, will be gathered on the Day of Resurrection, so let us respond positively to the Divine Messengers. «استجیبوا للّه وللرّسول... الیه تحشرون»

473) Tafsir Al-Mizan

Ayah

وَٱتَّقُوا۟ فِتْنَةً لَّا تُصِيبَنَّ ٱلَّذِينَ ظَلَمُوا۟ مِنكُمْ خَآصَّةً ۖ وَٱعْلَمُوٓا۟ أَنَّ ٱللَّهَ شَدِيدُ ٱلْعِقَابِ (۲٥)
And beware of a punishment, which shall not visit the wrongdoers among you exclusively, and know that Allah is severe in retribution. (25)

Key Points

Fitnah (trial/tribulation) means polytheism (shirk), disbelief (kufr), calamity and test, torture, and punishment. In this ayah, it refers to social calamities and afflictions. [474]

In the previous ayah, the command was to obey the Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله; here, it is to beware of fitnah. Thus, it becomes clear that one of the manifestations of fitnah is disobedience to the Prophet, and the concept of this ayah is identical to the ayah: «واعتصموا بحبل اللّه جمیعاً و لا تفرّقوا» [475]. [476]

Another manifestation of fitnah is corruption (fasad) and immorality (fahsha), which cause the collapse of a system, and their harm is general and widespread. An example is the corruption of the Umayyad rulers (Bani Umayyah) who, by setting aside the guardianship (wilayah) of the rightful leader, subjected Muslims to humiliation for centuries.

Silence and the abandonment of commanding the good and forbidding the evil (Amr bil Ma’ruf wa Nahy anil Munkar) cause fitnah and the spread of punishment. As the Prophet said: Allah never punishes all people for the sins of some of them, unless abominable acts become manifest among them and others remain silent despite having the ability to denounce and confront them. If this happens, Allah punishes all of them (the specific perpetrators and the general populace). [477] Similarly, Imam Ali عليه السلام said: If a sin is committed secretly, Allah does not punish the general public, but if a group openly commits abominable acts and the people do not reproach them, both groups deserve punishment. [478]

The meaning of avoiding actions that cause fitnah and affect everyone is not isolation and withdrawal from issues, but rather it conveys the necessity of human vigilance and attention. Imam Ali عليه السلام says: In times of fitnah, be like a young camel (Ibn al-Labun) that neither carries a burden for you, nor is milked by you, nor is exploited by you. «كن فى الفتنة كابن اللبون، لا ظهر فیركب و لا ضرع فیحلب» [479]

Upon the revelation of this ayah, the Noble Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله said: Whoever commits injustice regarding the succession of Ali عليه السلام after me, «فكانّما جحد نبوّتى و نبوة الانبیاء قبلى» (it is) as if he has denied my prophethood and the prophethood of the Prophets before me. [480]
