Messages

1- Deviation from religion and divine commands is the cause of the emergence of fitnah and the descent of divine punishment. «دعاكم لما یحییكم... واتّقوا فتنة...»

2- We should neither become agents of fitnah ourselves, nor side with those who cause fitnah, nor remain silent in the face of them. «واتّقوا فتنة»

3- The destiny of individuals and societies depends on their own actions. «واتّقوافتنةلاتصیبنّ»

4- Members of the believing community must be committed not only to their own actions but also to the actions of others, because sometimes the consequences of wrongful deeds affect others as well, just like someone who drills a hole in his spot on a ship. «و اتّقوا فتنة لاتصیبنّ الّذین ظلموا منكم خاصّة»

5- Attention to the severity and harshness of divine punishment prepares the ground for avoiding oppression (zulm) and sin. «و اتّقوا فتنة... انّ اللّه شدید العقاب»

Ayah

وَٱذْكُرُوٓا۟ إِذْ أَنتُمْ قَلِيلٌ مُّسْتَضْعَفُونَ فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ تَخَافُونَ أَن يَتَخَطَّفَكُمُ ٱلنَّاسُ فَـَٔاوَىٰكُمْ وَأَيَّدَكُم بِنَصْرِهِۦ وَرَزَقَكُم مِّنَ ٱلطَّيِّبَـٰتِ لَعَلَّكُمْ تَشْكُرُونَ (۲٦)
Remember when you were few, abased in the land, and feared lest the people should despoil you, and He gave you refuge, and strengthened you with His help, and provided you with all the good things so that you may give thanks. (26)

Key Points

«تَخطّف» means seizing and snatching away quickly. Before the Prophet’s migration to Medina, the Muslims in Mecca were constantly subjected to harassment, persecution, and torture by the polytheists. Therefore, upon the Prophet’s advice and planning, they migrated individually or in groups to other regions; some went to Abyssinia, some to Yemen and Ta'if, and some went to Shi'b Abi Talib, and they were constantly in fear and anxiety. When they migrated to Medina, they had no homes or shelter, and a large number of them were among the Aṣḥāb aṣ-Ṣuffah residing in the Prophet's Mosque. Furthermore, in the early days of the Prophet’s migration to Medina, the Ansar (Helpers) were afflicted by poverty, destitution, and famine, to the extent that they subsisted on a single date. They were also subjected to harassment by the Jews. However, towards the end of the Prophetic mission, Allah granted the Muslims capability and power, and they reached such strength that the Ahl al-Dhimmah (People of the Covenant) paid them Jizyah (tribute), or the polytheists were constantly in fear and anxiety of being killed, captured, or enslaved by the Muslims, and some willingly offered their wealth to the Muslims. (481) Here, Allah reminds them of their weakness and inability so that they may be grateful for the existing power and security.

(481) Tafsir Atyab al-Bayan

Messages

  1. Remembering the days of weakness and the days of Allah's help is a means of avoiding trials (fitnah). «واتّقوا فتنة - واذكروا»
  2. Initially, the number of Muslims was very small. (The word qaleel [قلیل] instead of qaleeloon [قلیلون] emphasizes the scarcity.)
  3. Before the migration to Medina, Muslims in Mecca constantly lived in fear and anxiety. «تخافون» (The imperfect verb form indicates continuity/duration.)
  4. In the path of truth, we should not fear the small number of people, weakness, or displacement, «واذكروا اذ انتم قلیل», for Allah's help is certain. «فاواكم و ایّدكم...»
  5. Security «فاواكم», military capability and victory «ایّدكم بنصره», and the improvement of economic resources «رزقكم من الطیّبات», are among the special divine blessings.
  6. Remembering the days of weakness and comparing them with the days of victory leads to gratitude, love, and reliance upon Allah. «واذكروا... لعلّكم تشكرون»
  7. In the shade of faith, Allah grants honor, power, and capability to the small, weak, and fearful group. (The entire Ayah)

Ayah

يَـٰٓأَيُّهَا ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ لَا تَخُونُوا۟ ٱللَّهَ وَٱلرَّسُولَ وَتَخُونُوٓا۟ أَمَـٰنَـٰتِكُمْ وَأَنتُمْ تَعْلَمُونَ (۲۷)
O you who have faith! Do not betray Allah and the Apostle, and do not betray your trusts knowingly. (27)

Key Points

Regarding the occasion of revelation (Shān Nuzūl) of this Ayah, it is narrated that when the Muslims besieged the Jews of Banī Qurayẓah by the command of the Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله, they proposed peace and migration to Syria. The Prophet did not accept this and appointed Sa'd ibn Mu'ādh for arbitration. One of the Muslims accompanying him, named Abū Lubābah, who had a history of friendship with them, signaled to them by pointing to his throat that if they accepted the arbitration of Sa'd ibn Mu'ādh, they would all be killed. Gabriel informed the Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله of this gesture. After that, Abū Lubābah, ashamed of this betrayal, tied himself to a pillar of the Prophet's Mosque and did not eat anything for seven days and nights. Finally, Allah accepted his repentance. [482] Another occasion of revelation has also been mentioned: during the Battle of Badr, some Muslims wrote a letter to Abū Sufyān and informed him of the Prophet's صلى الله علیه وآله plan. Abū Sufyān then requested help from the polytheists of Mecca, and a thousand men set out for the Battle of Badr. [483]

In the culture of the Quran, trust (amānah) has a broad meaning and encompasses all aspects of political, social, and moral life, including: A: All material and spiritual blessings, such as the school (of thought), the Quran, the leader, children, water, and land. B: The Ahl al-Bayt of the Prophet علیهم السلام. [484] C: We ourselves are a trust for ourselves, just as we read in another Ayah: «انّكم كنتم تختانون أنفسكم» [485], and society is also a trust for us. D: Anfāl (public wealth), spoils of war (ghanā'im), Khums, Zakāt, and public funds. (The Ayah's inclusion in Sūrat al-Anfāl and the spoils of the Battle of Badr). E: Imam al-Bāqir عليه السلام said: The rulings of the religion, the divine obligations (farā'iḍ), and duties are a trust. [486] F: Governance and responsibility.

Therefore, disobedience and failure to fulfill one's rights and duties concerning these trusts constitute betrayal (khiyānah). As Ibn 'Abbās says: Whoever abandons any part of the Islamic programs has committed a type of betrayal against Allah and the Prophet. [487] Similarly, the precedence of the unworthy over the worthy in social responsibilities is a betrayal of Allah, the Messenger, and the Muslims.

The Noble Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله said: He who betrays a trust is in reality a hypocrite, even if he fasts and prays and considers himself a Muslim. And Imam al-Sādiq عليه السلام said: Do not be deceived by people's prayers and fasting, for sometimes all of that is merely habit. Test and recognize people by their truthfulness and the fulfillment of trust. [488]

Non-betrayal of trust is among human duties and rights; therefore, one must not betray the trust even of a non-Muslim.

482) Tafsīr Majma' al-Bayān, Sāfī, and Namūneh 483) Tafsīr Majma' al-Bayān and Al-Mīzān 484) Mulḥaqāt Iḥqāq al-Ḥaqq, Vol. 14, p. 564 485) Al-Baqarah, 187 486) Tafsīr Nūr al-Thaqalayn 487) Tafsīr Namūneh 488) Kāfī, Vol. 2, p. 104

Messages

1- Betrayal is incompatible with faith. The prerequisite of faith is trustworthiness. «یا ایها الّذین آمنوا لاتخونوا»

2- Sometimes a mere gesture benefiting the enemy constitutes betrayal. (Considering the first occasion of revelation)

3- Revealing military secrets is a grave betrayal. (Considering the second occasion of revelation of the Ayah)

4- Betrayal of the Prophet is betrayal of Allah. «لاتخونوا اللّه و الرّسول» (The prohibition «لاتخونوا» [do not betray] is mentioned jointly for Allah and the Prophet, and then repeated regarding other people.)

5- Betrayal of Allah and the Messenger is betrayal of oneself, and its harm returns to the person himself. «لاتخونوا اللّه والرّسول وتخونوا اماناتكم» (According to this meaning, which is consistent with the apparent sense of the Ayah, there is no need to assume the omission of the word 'لا' [not] before «تخونوا اماناتكم».)

6- The trust of the people is equivalent to our own trust, and betraying it is betraying ourselves. «لا تخونوا... تخونوا اماناتكم»

7- Betrayal is inherently ugly and condemned. «و انتم تعلمون»

8- Conscious betrayals are dangerous. «و تخونوا اماناتكم و انتم تعلمون» (You are aware both of the ugliness of betrayal and of its negative consequences.)

Ayah

وَٱعْلَمُوٓا۟ أَنَّمَآ أَمْوَٰلُكُمْ وَأَوْلَـٰدُكُمْ فِتْنَةٌ وَأَنَّ ٱللَّهَ عِندَهُۥٓ أَجْرٌ عَظِيمٌ (۲۸)
Know that your possessions and children are only a test, and that Allah—with Him is a great reward. (28)

Key Points

The central axis of many human errors and the root of many sins—such as unlawful transactions, hoarding, lying, short-changing, abandoning charity (infaq), withholding Khums and Zakat, greed, obstructionism, false oaths, violating people's rights, fleeing the battlefield, abandoning migration (Hijra) and Jihad, etc.—is the attachment to wealth and children. Thus, these are factors for trial (imtihan) and error, just as they led Abu Lubaba (in the previous Ayah) into error and compelled him to cooperate with the enemy to protect his wealth and children. Those who informed Abu Sufyan from Medina that the Muslims had mobilized forces to seize their trade caravan were among the Muhajirun (emigrants) who had wealth and children in Mecca. (489)

The Quran has warned, using various expressions, about wealth and children being a trial (fitnah), about not becoming attached to them, and about the possibility of deviation through them, including: A: The possibility of Satan sharing in man's wealth and children. «شاركهم فى الاموال و الاولاد» (490) B: Seeking increase (competition) in wealth and children and its negative effects. «تكاثر فى الاموال و الاولاد» (491) C: Wealth and children distracting from the remembrance of Allah. «لا تلهكم أموالكم و لا أولادكم عن ذكر اللّه» (492) D: Wealth and children being unable to save (them) on the Day of Judgment. «لن تغنى عنهم أموالهم و لا أولادهم» (493)

Several points in the Ayah indicate that the trial involving wealth and children is very difficult and serious, and succeeding in it is extremely important. Firstly, the Ayah begins with the phrase «واعلموا» (Know), which is a warning. Secondly, the word «انّما» (only/indeed) shows that the trial involving wealth and children is certain and unavoidable. Thirdly, wealth and children themselves are considered fitnah (trial/temptation), not merely tools for trial and temptation. (494)

Imam Ali عليه السلام said: «لایقولنّ احدكم الّلهم انّى اعوذ بك من الفتنة لانّه لیس احد الاّ و هو مشتمل على فتنة» (Let none of you say, 'O Allah, I seek refuge in You from the trial/fitnah,' because there is no one who is not encompassed by trial). No one should ask Allah to be kept away from trial and test, because all people, without exception, will face it. Rather, one should ask Allah for protection from error and deviation during the trials. Then, the Imam spoke about the reason and philosophy behind divine tests, saying: It is so that the content (satisfied) person may be distinguished from the discontented, and so that the factors leading to the happiness (sa'adah) or wretchedness (shaqawah) of each person become clear through his deeds. (495)

489) Tafsir Al-Mizan 490) Isra, 64 491) Hadid, 20 492) Munafiqun, 9 493) Aal Imran, 10 494) Tafsir Rahnamā 495) Nahj al-Balaghah, Maxim 93

Messages

1- Excessive attachment to wealth and children leads a person to treachery. «لا تخونوا... و اعلموا...» (Do not betray... and Know...) and sometimes even causes one to abandon human and religious principles (trust and fulfilling the trust). (Considering the occasion of revelation of the previous Ayah)

2- Wealth and children can be two deceptive traps. «انّما اموالكم و اولادكم فتنة»

3- Attention to the great divine reward is among the means of detaching oneself from the world and abandoning treachery. «اَنّ اللّه عنده أجر عظیم»

4- Wealth and children, despite all their attractions, are insignificant compared to the favors and rewards of Allah. (The phrase «اجر عظیم» (great reward) is mentioned in contrast to «اموالكم و اولادكم» (your wealth and your children)). (496)

496) As I write these lines, it is the night of Husayni Ashura, when Imam Husayn عليه السلام traded everything He possessed with Allah in a single day and witnessed tragedies, each of which was sufficient to break a person. But what kept the Imam steadfast against all events was that He considered them insignificant before Allah. Even when His six-month-old child was martyred before Him, He said: "The fact that Allah is watching this scene is sufficient for me." And when Lady Zaynab عليها السلام saw the mutilated body of her brother, Imam Husayn عليه السلام, on the afternoon of Ashura, she said: "O Allah! Accept this small offering from us." And in the court of Yazid, she also said: "I saw nothing in Karbala but beauty (Jamil) from Allah." Yes, by believing in the scarcity and transience of the world, and by believing in the greatness and the great reward (Ajr Azim) near Allah, one can confront the most difficult events.

Ayah

يَـٰٓأَيُّهَا ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوٓا۟ إِن تَتَّقُوا۟ ٱللَّهَ يَجْعَل لَّكُمْ فُرْقَانًا وَيُكَفِّرْ عَنكُمْ سَيِّـَٔاتِكُمْ وَيَغْفِرْ لَكُمْ ۗ وَٱللَّهُ ذُو ٱلْفَضْلِ ٱلْعَظِيمِ (۲۹)
O you who have faith! If you are wary of Allah, He shall appoint a criterion for you, and absolve you of your misdeeds, and forgive you, for Allah is dispenser of a great grace. (29)

Key Points

The means and criteria for recognizing truth (Haqq) from falsehood (Batil) are numerous, including: A: Divine Prophets and Awliya (saints/guardians); just as the Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله was named Faruq [497], and as we read in a Hadith: «مَن فارق علیّاً فقد فارق اللّه» [498] (Whoever separates from Ali عليه السلام has separated from Allah). B: The Heavenly Book; by presenting matters to it, one can distinguish truth from falsehood. C: Piety (Taqwa), because the storm of instincts and the loves and hatreds accompanied by lack of piety prevent the comprehension of realities. D: Intellect and wisdom (Aql and Khirad); without which one cannot even approach revelation (Wahy).

Furqan (the criterion) and the power to distinguish truth from falsehood is a God-given wisdom and insight bestowed upon the people of piety, and it is not dependent on academic knowledge, literacy, or information.

According to Tafsir Kabir by Fakhr Razi, "تكفیر سیّئات" (expiation of evil deeds) is covering up sins in this world, and "مغفرت" (forgiveness) is deliverance from Allah's wrath in the Hereafter. According to Tafsir Namuneh, "Takfir" is the erasure of the social and psychological effects of sin, and "Maghfirat" is pardon regarding the torment of Hellfire.

Those who possess piety and set aside carnal desires are granted the power to recognize the truth. As we read in another Ayah: «اتّقوا اللّه و یعلّمكم اللّه» [499] (Be conscious of Allah, and Allah will teach you).

It is as if the soul is like a mirror from which piety wipes away the dust, and the light of truth is reflected therein. Just as Hadrat Ali عليه السلام considers piety to be the medicine and cure for hearts, the light and sight for blindness of the heart, the healing and means of reforming the heart, purifying the soul, security from inner fears, and the illumination of darknesses: «فانّ تقوى اللّه دواء داء قلوبكم و بصر عمى افئدتكم و شفاء مرض اجسادكم و صلاح فساد صدوركم و طهور دنس انفسكم و جلاء عشا ابصاركم و أمن مفزع جأشكم و ضیاء سواد ظلمتكم...» [500]

Conversely, greed and avarice cause the intellects to slip. Hadrat Ali عليه السلام says: «اكثر مصارع العقول تحت بروق المطامع» [501] (The greatest stumbling blocks for intellects are beneath the flashes of avarice).

Reality is an adorned abode, But lust and desire raise dust. Do you not see that wherever dust rises, The sight cannot perceive, even if the man is sighted.

497) Tafsir Furqan 498) Mulhaqat Ihqaq al-Haqq, Vol. 4, p. 26 499) Al-Baqarah, 282 500) Nahj al-Balaghah, Sermon 189 501) Nahj al-Balaghah, Maxim 219

Messages

1- If we possess piety, Allah guides us on how to deal with the trial of wealth and children. «اموالكم و اولادكم فتنة... ان تتّقوا اللّه یجعل لكم فرقانا»

2- Human actions affect his insight. The person who has piety, in addition to knowledge, innate disposition (Fitrah), intellect, and experience, is granted a special insight, vision, and recognition by Allah. (The word "فرقان" is indefinite (nakirah) to indicate that a specific insight is intended.)

3- Piety is both the factor for correct recognition, «فرقان», and the source of social honor and standing, «یُكفّر عنكم», and the cause of forgiveness in the Hereafter. «یغفر لكم»

4- The granting of special insight to the pious and the forgiveness of their sins is a favor (Tafaddul) from Allah. «یجعل لكم فرقانا... واللّه ذو الفضل العظیم»

Ayah

وَإِذْ يَمْكُرُ بِكَ ٱلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا۟ لِيُثْبِتُوكَ أَوْ يَقْتُلُوكَ أَوْ يُخْرِجُوكَ ۚ وَيَمْكُرُونَ وَيَمْكُرُ ٱللَّهُ ۖ وَٱللَّهُ خَيْرُ ٱلْمَـٰكِرِينَ (۳٠)
When the faithless plotted against you to take you captive, or to kill or expel you—they plotted and Allah devised, and Allah is the best of devisers. (30)

Key Points

Makr (plotting/cunning) means planning, strategizing, design, and scheme. This Ayah refers to the "Night of the Stay" (Laylat al-Mabīt) and the conspiracy of the disbelievers to kill the Messenger of Allah صلى الله علیه وآله, of which the Prophet was informed through Gabriel. Ali عليه السلام slept in his place in the bed, and the Prophet left the house at night and went to the Cave of Thawr, and from there migrated to Medina. Furthermore, on the night of the Hijra (migration), because Ali عليه السلام was ready to sacrifice his life for the Prophet, the Ayah "And among the people is he who sells himself seeking the pleasure of Allah" [502] was revealed concerning Imam Ali عليه السلام. [503] The three plans and schemes mentioned in the Ayah for the disbelievers to confront the Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله were the result of the polytheists' meeting in Dār an-Nadwah (the Council House), and finally, they approved the plan of assassination: that one person from every tribe should be ready to attack collectively and kill the Prophet, so that His relatives could not engage in conflict with all the tribes for revenge. This Ayah might be related to Ayah 27 of the same Surah, which stated: Do not betray Allah and the Messenger, for if you betray, you will be exposed by Divine planning. It is often the case that Allah accomplishes important matters through a weak creature or thwarts very large conspiracies with it: the children of Adam are taught by a crow, the death of Solomon is discovered by a termite, Bilqis is invited by a hoopoe, the elephant riders of Abrahah are destroyed by small birds called Abābīl, and Nimrod by a mosquito, and the conspiracy of the disbelievers of Mecca is thwarted by a spider. All of this is to demonstrate the ultimate weakness and inability of humanity before the endless Divine power.

502) Baqarah, 207 503) Tafsir Nur ath-Thaqalayn

Messages

1- Remembering Divine aid and the dangers that Allah has kept away from us is a source of tranquility and strength of heart. «و اذ یمكر بك...» 2- Imprisonment, assassination, and exile are the tools used by the tyrants of history to confront the truth. «لیثبتوك أو یقتلوك...» 3- Disbelief leads a person to the extent of prophet-killing. «الّذین كفروا... یقتلوك» 4- In the face of the truth, there is no argument or logic. Instead of solving the issue and accepting the truth, the disbelievers act to eliminate the subject of truth and the men of truth. «الّذین كفروا لیثبتوك او یقتلوك او...» 5- The conspiracy of the disbelievers against the Muslims and Allah's support for the Muslims are constant. «یمكرون و یمكر اللّه» (It is not the case that enemies always achieve their goals; Allah thwarts their conspiracies through His planning.) 6- Whoever schemes against the proponents of truth is dealing with Allah. «یمكرون و یمكر اللّه» 7- Allah is the Protector of His Book and His Awliyā' (saints/friends); He protects the noblest of creation with a spider's web, thwarts the greatest conspiracies, and changes the course of history. «واللّه خیر الماكرین» 8- Wherever necessary, Allah informs His Awliyā' of the conspiracies. A plan that they wanted no one to know about became known to the whole world. «واللّه خیر الماكرین»

Ayah

وَإِذَا تُتْلَىٰ عَلَيْهِمْ ءَايَـٰتُنَا قَالُوا۟ قَدْ سَمِعْنَا لَوْ نَشَآءُ لَقُلْنَا مِثْلَ هَـٰذَآ ۙ إِنْ هَـٰذَآ إِلَّآ أَسَـٰطِيرُ ٱلْأَوَّلِينَ (۳۱)
When Our signs are recited to them, they say, ‘We have heard already. If we want, we [too] can say like this. These are nothing but myths of the ancients.’ (31)

Key Points

| In the previous Ayah, the discussion was about the continuous plots of the disbelievers and their conspiracy to expel and kill the Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله. This Ayah describes an example of their cultural plots designed to belittle the school of thought (Islam) and the Quran. "أساطیر" (Asātīr) is the plural of "اسطوره" (ustūrah), meaning superstitious and imaginary legends, which are either about the early humans or are fabricated by them. Nadr ibn al-Harith, during a trip to Iran, learned the stories of Rustam and Isfandiyar. Upon returning to Hijaz, he recounted them to the people and claimed that he too could speak and tell stories like Muhammad صلى الله علیه وآله. [504]

504) Tafsirs Majma‘ al-Bayan, Ruh al-Ma‘ani, and Fi Zilal al-Quran

Messages

1- Among the weapons of the enemy are demagoguery (deceiving the masses), belittling, and mocking the truth. «قالوا قد سمعنا لو نشاء لقلنا مثل هذا» (They said: We have heard; if we wished, we could certainly say the like of this). However, the enemies are empty drums; they only make claims, and in practice, they are incapable of producing anything similar to the Quran.

2- Even the enemies admitted the superiority and extraordinary nature of the Quran. «ان هذا الاّ أساطیر الاوّلین» (This is nothing but tales of the ancients).

3- The accusation of stagnation (tahajjur), traditionalism, and worshipping the old is among the oldest accusations leveled against the believers. «أساطیر الاوّلین» (tales of the ancients).

Ayah

وَإِذْ قَالُوا۟ ٱللَّهُمَّ إِن كَانَ هَـٰذَا هُوَ ٱلْحَقَّ مِنْ عِندِكَ فَأَمْطِرْ عَلَيْنَا حِجَارَةً مِّنَ ٱلسَّمَآءِ أَوِ ٱئْتِنَا بِعَذَابٍ أَلِيمٍ (۳۲)
And when they said, ‘O Allah, if this be the truth from You, rain down upon us stones from the sky, or bring us a painful punishment.’ (32)

Key Points

Such curses are either due to extreme fanaticism and obstinacy, or they are for deceiving the masses. They curse themselves so that simple-hearted people feel that they (the cursers) are right and Islam is false. Following the appointment of Hadrat Ali عليه السلام to the Imamate by the Noble Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله, which took place by the command of Allah at Ghadir Khumm, and the Prophet said: «مَن كنت مولاه فهذا علىّ مولاه» (Whoever I am his Master, Ali is his Master), Nu'man ibn Harith, who was one of the hypocrites, came to the Prophet and said: You commanded us regarding Monotheism (Tawhid), Prophethood (Nubuwwah), Jihad, Hajj, fasting, prayer (Salat), and Zakat, and we accepted. Now you have appointed this young man as our Imam?! The Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله said: It was by the command of God. Out of extreme distress, he cursed himself, and in his curse, he drew inspiration from this very Ayah. [505] However, he had not yet taken a few steps when a stone fell upon his head from the sky and destroyed him, and the first verses of Surah Al-Ma'arij were revealed. [506] «سئل سائل بعذاب واقع»

505) Al-Ghadir, Vol. 1, pp. 239 to 266, quoting 30 scholars from the Ahl al-Sunnah 506) Tafsir Majma' al-Bayan

Messages

1- Sometimes feigning piety and swearing oaths are among the enemy's tactics to deceive the believers. «الّلهم...»

2- Enmity and obstinacy lead a person to the point of consenting to his own destruction. «فامطر علینا حجارة...»

3- Some opponents of the Prophet believed in God (Allah), His influence in existence, and the effectiveness of supplication (Du'a). «الّلهم ان كان... امطر علینا... او ائتنا...»

Ayah

وَمَا كَانَ ٱللَّهُ لِيُعَذِّبَهُمْ وَأَنتَ فِيهِمْ ۚ وَمَا كَانَ ٱللَّهُ مُعَذِّبَهُمْ وَهُمْ يَسْتَغْفِرُونَ (۳۳)
But Allah will not punish them while you are in their midst, nor will Allah punish them while they plead for forgiveness. (33)

Key Points

Considering that in the next Ayah, the disbelievers of Mecca are threatened with punishment, it becomes clear that the purpose of negating the punishment in this Ayah is the removal of general punishment from the Muslims due to the blessing of the presence of the Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله, similar to the punishments of previous nations. Otherwise, individuals have been afflicted by divine punishment in specific cases, such as the punishment of Nu'man ibn Harith in the previous Ayah.

It is mentioned in Ahadith that Allah removes hardship and punishment from others due to the presence of certain pure individuals and divine scholars. For instance, in the story of the destruction of the people of Lot, Prophet Abraham said to the angels commissioned for the punishment: «انّ فیها لوطاً» (Indeed, Lot is in it). Meaning, would you destroy that place despite the presence of a godly man in the area?! The angels replied: We know that Lot is there, and We have commanded him to leave that place. (507)

Or, Imam Ali عليه السلام said after the passing of the Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله: One of the two safeguards has departed from among us; preserve the other safeguard, which is seeking forgiveness (Istighfar). (508)

Or, we read in a narration that Imam Reza عليه السلام told Zakariyya ibn Adam: Remain in the city of Qom, for just as Allah removed affliction and punishment from the people of Baghdad through Imam Kadhim عليه السلام, He will also keep affliction away from that city through your presence. (509)

The Noble Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله said: My life and my death are good for you; during my lifetime, Allah removes punishment from you, and after my death, by presenting your deeds to me, you will be covered by goodness through my seeking forgiveness and pardon for you. «امّا فى مماتى فتعرض علىّ اعمالكم فاستغفر لكم» (510)

Disobedience and committing certain sins are among the causes for the descent of divine affliction and punishment, and the way to compensate for this is repentance (Tawbah) and seeking forgiveness (Istighfar). «و ما كان اللّه معذّبهم و هم یستغفرون» As we read in Dua Kumayl: «اللّهم اغفر لى الذنوب الّتى تنزل البلاء» (O Allah, forgive me the sins that bring down affliction). And just as Allah says in another Ayah: «و ما كان ربّك لیهلك القرى بظلم و أهلها مصلحون» (511) As long as the people are righteous and engaged in reform, Allah will not destroy them.

Messages

1- The presence of the Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله and the men of God is a source of security (Aman) for the inhabitants of the earth. «و ما كان اللّه لیعذّبهم و أنت فیهم»

2- Seeking forgiveness (Istighfar) prevents affliction. «و ما كان اللّه معذّبهم و هم یستغفرون» (Returning from disbelief and atheism is also a type of Istighfar.)

3- Repentance (Tawbah) and seeking forgiveness (Istighfar) hold special importance with Allah, such that they can change the destiny of a nation. «و ما كان اللّه معذّبهم و هم یستغفرون»

Ayah

وَمَا لَهُمْ أَلَّا يُعَذِّبَهُمُ ٱللَّهُ وَهُمْ يَصُدُّونَ عَنِ ٱلْمَسْجِدِ ٱلْحَرَامِ وَمَا كَانُوٓا۟ أَوْلِيَآءَهُۥٓ ۚ إِنْ أَوْلِيَآؤُهُۥٓ إِلَّا ٱلْمُتَّقُونَ وَلَـٰكِنَّ أَكْثَرَهُمْ لَا يَعْلَمُونَ (۳٤)
What [excuse] have they that Allah should not punish them, when they bar [the faithful] from the Holy Mosque, and they are not its custodians? Its custodians are only the Godwary, but most of them do not know. (34)

Key Points

In the previous Ayah, Allah, due to the presence of the Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله among the people, and also due to their return, repentance, and seeking forgiveness, removed from them a punishment like the heavenly punishment of the people of 'Ad and Thamud. However, in this Ayah, the discussion is about their punishment, which suggests that they deserve the punishment, even though Allah does not punish them because of the presence of the Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله. Of course, it is possible that the intended meaning of punishment in this Ayah refers to the calamities and hardships of war and captivity.

Imam Ali عليه السلام says: «اللّه اللّه فى بیت ربّكم لا تخلوه مابقیتم فانّه ان تُرك لم تناظروا» [512], Fear Allah, fear Allah, concerning the House of your Lord, for if you abandon it and it is neglected, the Divine punishment will immediately overtake you.

512) Nahj al-Balaghah, Letter 47

Messages

1- Those who prevent believers from entering the Sacred Mosque (Masjid al-Haram) are impious individuals and must await Divine punishment. «و ما لهم ألاّ یعذّبهم...»

2- The disbelievers wrongfully considered themselves the guardians (custodians) of the Sacred Mosque. «وماكانوا اولیائه»

3- The guardianship of a House built by the hand of Abraham, the Friend of Allah (Ibrahim Khalil) عليه السلام, and founded upon piety (taqwa), must not be in the hands of impious individuals. «انْ أولیائه الاّ المتّقون»

4- Appointing a guardian (custodian) to manage the affairs of the Sacred Mosque is necessary. «انْ أولیائه الاّ المتّقون» (Since the Ayah states the conditions for the custodian, rather than the principle of custodianship itself, appointing a custodian is mandatory.)

Ayah

وَمَا كَانَ صَلَاتُهُمْ عِندَ ٱلْبَيْتِ إِلَّا مُكَآءً وَتَصْدِيَةً ۚ فَذُوقُوا۟ ٱلْعَذَابَ بِمَا كُنتُمْ تَكْفُرُونَ (۳٥)
Their prayer at the House is nothing but whistling and clapping. So taste the punishment because of what you used to defy. (35)

Key Points

The term «مُكاء» means whistling, and «تَصدیة» means clapping. Their whistling was perhaps intended to announce their presence to the idols that were placed in the Ka'bah. Commentators consider the defeat of the polytheists in the Battle of Badr to be a clear instance of the punishment mentioned in the phrase «فذوقوا العذاب». [513]

513) Tafasir Majma' al-Bayan, Al-Mizan, and Fi Zilal al-Qur'an

Messages

  1. Disrespect and engaging in vain and useless acts, such as clapping and whistling in a sacred place, indicate a lack of qualification for the guardianship (custodianship) of that place. «انْ اولیائه الاّ المتّقون... و ما كان صلاتهم عند البیت الاّ مُكاء وتصدیة»

  2. Throughout history, religious ceremonies have been subjected to distortion or mixed with vain acts. «صلاتهم... مُكاءً و تصدیة»

  3. Sometimes the most sacred centers become the scene of the highest superstitions. «عند البیت الا مكاءً و تصدیة»

  4. Disbelief and disrespect toward sacred things lead to divine punishment. «مكاء و تصدیة فذوقوا العذاب بما كنتم تكفرون»

  5. Obstinacy and persistence in disbelief and deviation are factors leading to the descent of punishment. «فذوقوا العذاب بما كنتم تكفرون»

Ayah

إِنَّ ٱلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا۟ يُنفِقُونَ أَمْوَٰلَهُمْ لِيَصُدُّوا۟ عَن سَبِيلِ ٱللَّهِ ۚ فَسَيُنفِقُونَهَا ثُمَّ تَكُونُ عَلَيْهِمْ حَسْرَةً ثُمَّ يُغْلَبُونَ ۗ وَٱلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوٓا۟ إِلَىٰ جَهَنَّمَ يُحْشَرُونَ (۳٦)
Indeed the faithless spend their wealth to bar from the way of Allah. Soon they will have spent it, then it will be a cause of regret to them, then they will be overcome, and the faithless will be gathered toward Hell, (36)

Key Points

Some believe the revelation of this Ayah concerns the heavy budget that the disbelievers of Mecca spent for the Battle of Badr, but the generality of the Ayah includes all budgets spent on fighting and confronting Islam. The repetition of the phrase "الّذین كفروا" (those who disbelieved) is perhaps because some of the investing disbelievers later became Muslim and regretted the money they had spent, while others remained in disbelief and became inhabitants of Hell. Yes, Hell is for the disbelievers who remain in disbelief, but believers, because they work and invest for Allah, do not regret it even if they do not achieve the desired result, because Allah grants them their reward.

Messages

1- Disbelievers constantly spend their wealth to prevent the spread of Islam, but ultimately, the result is nothing but regret and certain defeat. (The entire Ayah)

2- The Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله gives a hidden (unseen) report that they will invest against Islam in the future as well, but victory belongs to Islam. «فسینفقونها... ثمّ تغلبون»

3- The efforts of the disbelievers result not only in worldly defeat but also in punishment in the Hereafter. «یغلبون... الى جهنّم یُحشَرون»

4- Lack of faith and disbelief (kufr) cause the downfall of man and lead to entry into Hell. «والّذین كفروا الى جهنّم یُحشرون»

Ayah

لِيَمِيزَ ٱللَّهُ ٱلْخَبِيثَ مِنَ ٱلطَّيِّبِ وَيَجْعَلَ ٱلْخَبِيثَ بَعْضَهُۥ عَلَىٰ بَعْضٍ فَيَرْكُمَهُۥ جَمِيعًا فَيَجْعَلَهُۥ فِى جَهَنَّمَ ۚ أُو۟لَـٰٓئِكَ هُمُ ٱلْخَـٰسِرُونَ (۳۷)
so that Allah may separate the bad ones from the good, and place the bad on one another, and pile them up together, and cast them into hell. It is they who are the losers. (37)

Key Points

The word «یَركُمَه» means compacting and piling them up. Although Hell is very large and constantly says, "هل من مزید" [514] (Is there any more?) and demands more, every one of the inhabitants of Hell lives in confinement. This is like a large wall that has space for many nails, but every nail is under pressure.

514) Qaf, 30

Messages

1- One of the results of the war between truth (Haqq) and falsehood (Batil) is the clarification of temperaments, motives, actions, commitments, and conspiracies, so that the essence of human beings may be revealed. «لیمیز اللّه»

2- Separating the adherents of truth from falsehood is one of the Divine traditions (Sunan). «لیمیز اللّه» Just as on the Day of Resurrection, it will be addressed: «وامتازوا الیوم ایّها المجرمون» [515]

3- Compaction, pressure, and tightness of space are characteristics of Hell and its inhabitants. «فیركمه جمیعاً فیجعله فى جهنّم»

4- The true loss for man is his consignment to Hell. «فى جهنّم اولئك هم الخاسرون»

515) Ya Sin, 59

Ayah

قُل لِّلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوٓا۟ إِن يَنتَهُوا۟ يُغْفَرْ لَهُم مَّا قَدْ سَلَفَ وَإِن يَعُودُوا۟ فَقَدْ مَضَتْ سُنَّتُ ٱلْأَوَّلِينَ (۳۸)
Say to the faithless, if they cease [their belligerence against the Muslims], what is already past shall be forgiven them. But if they resume [their hostilities], then the precedent of the predecessors has already come to pass. (38)

Key Points

Intihā’ (انتهاء) means accepting a prohibition (nahy), refraining, and not performing an action because it has been forbidden.

Messages

  1. The Prophet is commanded to convey the tradition (Sunnah) of Allah (forgiveness if they repent, and suppression and destruction if the disbelievers persist in obstinacy) to the people. «قل للّذین كفروا ان ینتهوا یغفر لهم... وان یعدوا...»

  2. The repentance (Tawbah) of the disbeliever and the irreligious is also accepted. «قل للّذین كفروا ان ینتهوا یغفر لهم»

  3. In Islam, the path of repentance and reform is always open to individuals; there is no dead end. «ان ینتهوا یغفر لهم»

  4. In judgment, the criterion is the current state of individuals, not their past. «قل للذین كفروا ان ینتهوا...»

  5. We should give people the opportunity to think, repent, and reconsider. «ان ینتهوا...»

  6. Islam is not warmongering; rather, it seeks reform. «ان ینتهوا یغفر لهم»

  7. Becoming Muslim erases previous sins and transgressions. «ان ینتهوا یغفر لهم ما قد سلف» Just as we read in the Hadith: «الاسلام یَجُبّ ما قبله». [516]

  8. Encouragement is placed alongside threat. «ان ینتهوا... ان یعودوا»

  9. Keeping the window of hope open should not be done in such a way that it feels as if we have approached the criminals out of weakness. «ان ینتهوا، ان یعودوا»

  10. First encouragement and completion of the proof (Itmām al-Hujjah), then threat and severity. «ان ینتهوا، ان یعودوا» (A Muslim considers himself responsible regarding social anomalies.)

  11. The just and wise Divine law has been uniform and unchangeable for everyone throughout history. «فقد مضت سنّت الاوّلین»

  12. The Divine tradition (Sunnah) among previous nations was the salvation and victory of the Prophets and the punishment of those who persisted in disbelief. «فقد مضت سنّت الاوّلین» Just as another Ayah states: «كتب اللّه لاغلبنّ اَنا و رسلى» [517]

  13. Let us learn lessons from the history and destiny of others. «فقد مضت سنّت الاوّلین»

Ayah

وَقَـٰتِلُوهُمْ حَتَّىٰ لَا تَكُونَ فِتْنَةٌ وَيَكُونَ ٱلدِّينُ كُلُّهُۥ لِلَّهِ ۚ فَإِنِ ٱنتَهَوْا۟ فَإِنَّ ٱللَّهَ بِمَا يَعْمَلُونَ بَصِيرٌ (۳۹)
Fight them until persecution is no more, and religion becomes exclusively for Allah. So if they desist, Allah indeed watches what they do. (39)

Key Points

War and Jihad in Islam are not for territorial expansion, but rather with the goal of spreading the school of Islam and confronting sedition/persecution (fitnah). In the Quran, fitnah is considered greater than killing, «الفتنة اكبر من القتل» [518], and even more severe than it. «الفتنة اشدّ من القتل» [519] Fitnah has a broad meaning and includes every kind of imposition of pressure. In the Quran, fitnah has also been used to mean polytheism (shirk), perhaps because various restrictions and pressures are imposed by the polytheists upon the thoughts and souls of truth-seekers and their community, or because polytheism leads to eternal punishment. Likewise, imposing disbelief (kufr) upon believers and people of pure nature is also fitnah. [520] According to Imam Sadiq عليه السلام, the definitive and ultimate realization of this Ayah will be during the time of Hadrat Mahdi عليه السلام. [521] Imam Baqir عليه السلام said: The interpretation (ta’wīl) of this Ayah has not yet come; when the time for the interpretation of the Ayah arrives, the perpetrators of fitnah will be killed and there will be no polytheism (shirk). [522] This Ayah does not contradict the freedom to accept religion: «لا اكراه فى الدّین» [523], because fitnah and tyranny (tāghūt) must be removed so that the space for human choice and free will is opened up.

518) Baqarah, 217 519) Baqarah, 191 520) Tafsir-e Nemūneh 521) Tafsir Nūr al-Thaqalayn 522) Kāfī, Vol. 8, p. 201 523) Baqarah, 256

Messages

1- The goal of Jihad in Islam is the removal of fitnah and the dominance of the disbelievers, and the creation of a healthy environment for inviting people to the truth. «قاتلوهم حتّى لاتكون فتنة و...»

2- As long as the disbelievers are engaged in creating fitnah, the command to fight exists. «قاتلوهم حتّى لاتكون فتنة»

3- Whenever the enemies cease fighting, they will be dealt with in a manner appropriate to that stage. «فان انتهوا...»

Ayah

وَإِن تَوَلَّوْا۟ فَٱعْلَمُوٓا۟ أَنَّ ٱللَّهَ مَوْلَىٰكُمْ ۚ نِعْمَ ٱلْمَوْلَىٰ وَنِعْمَ ٱلنَّصِيرُ (٤٠)
But if they turn away, then know that Allah is your master: an excellent master and an excellent helper! (40)

Messages

1- In managing military affairs and political planning, all aspects and conditions must be anticipated. «فان انتهوا - وان تولّوا»

2- You must fulfill your duty; if others fail to act or turn away, Allah is your Helper. «وان تولّوا فاعلموا انّ اللّه مولاكم»

3- Do not be heedless of divine guardianship and help. «فاعلموا» (Faith in Allah's guardianship and help leads to the growth and strengthening of the motivation to fight against seditionists, and the remembrance of His favors grants tranquility to the hearts of the believers against conspiracies and obstinacy.)

4- Allah is the best Protector (Mawla), because He neither entrusts us to another, nor forgets us, nor desires us for His own need, nor wastes the right or reward of anyone. «نعم المولى»

5- The help of Allah is superior to any other helper. «نعم النصیر»

Ayah

۞ وَٱعْلَمُوٓا۟ أَنَّمَا غَنِمْتُم مِّن شَىْءٍ فَأَنَّ لِلَّهِ خُمُسَهُۥ وَلِلرَّسُولِ وَلِذِى ٱلْقُرْبَىٰ وَٱلْيَتَـٰمَىٰ وَٱلْمَسَـٰكِينِ وَٱبْنِ ٱلسَّبِيلِ إِن كُنتُمْ ءَامَنتُم بِٱللَّهِ وَمَآ أَنزَلْنَا عَلَىٰ عَبْدِنَا يَوْمَ ٱلْفُرْقَانِ يَوْمَ ٱلْتَقَى ٱلْجَمْعَانِ ۗ وَٱللَّهُ عَلَىٰ كُلِّ شَىْءٍ قَدِيرٌ (٤۱)
Know that whatever thing you may come by, a fifth of it is for Allah and the Apostle, for the relatives and the orphans, for the needy and the traveller, if you have faith in Allah and what We sent down to Our servant on the Day of Separation, the day when the two hosts met; and Allah has power over all things. (41)

Key Points

The addressees of this Ayah, at the time of its revelation, are those who participated alongside the Prophet in the Battle of Badr and achieved victory through sacrifice and selflessness. They were people of prayer, fasting, migration (Hijrah), Jihad, and martyrdom, and the Prophet's hope rested upon them. Yet, despite this, Allah says in this Ayah: O Mujahideen of the Badr front, if you believe in Allah, the Prophet, and the Quran, give the Khums (one-fifth) of the spoils. This means that the condition for belief in Allah, besides prayer, fasting, and Jihad, is the payment of the obligatory financial duty, which is Khums.

The words "غنیمت" (Ghanimah/spoils) and "غرامت" (Gharamah/indemnity) each appear six times in the Quran. Just as Gharamah includes every kind of financial loss, not just war loss, Ghanimah also encompasses every kind of profit, not only war income. Lexicography books such as Lisan al-Arab, Taj al-Arus, and Qamus, and Sunni commentators such as Qurtubi, Fakhr Razi, and Alusi, have no doubt about the generality of the word. Similarly, in Mufradat by Raghib, it is stated: anything that a person acquires is called Ghanimah. In the Quran, Ghanimah has also been used for non-war spoils: «فعنداللّه مغانم كثیرة» [524] Imam Ali عليه السلام also states: «مَن اخذها لَحق و غَنم» [525], meaning, whoever acts according to the religion of Allah reaches his goal and profits.

According to narrations and the belief of the Shi’a, Ghanimah in this Ayah includes every kind of income, whether from earning, work, or trade. The revelation of the Ayah during the Battle of Badr does not mean that Khums is only applicable to war spoils. Even if the intent of Ghanimah in the Ayah were only war spoils, it must be said that one instance of Khums is mentioned in this Ayah, and other instances are discussed in the narrations. Khums holds special importance in the narrations, including the fact that whoever does not pay the Khums on his property, his property does not become lawful (Halal), and he cannot dispose of it, and prayer performed in clothing on which Khums has not been paid is problematic. [526]

This Ayah does not contradict the first Ayah, which considers Anfal (spoils) to belong solely to Allah and the Messenger: «قل الانفال للّه و للرسول», because, considering the Khums Ayah, those great figures (the Prophet or the Imam and his representative) keep the Khums, or one-fifth, of what they receive for the expenditures mentioned in the Ayah, and they give the rest (four-fifths) to the fighters.

Jurists consider Khums obligatory in seven things: 1. Profit and benefit from earning and annual income, 2. Treasure, 3. Minerals (Mines), 4. Jewels obtained through diving, 5. Lawful property mixed with unlawful property, 6. Land purchased by a Dhimmi Kafir from a Muslim, 7. War spoils. [527]

It is very clear that Allah has no need for Khums. Therefore, Allah's share is for the sovereignty of God's law and the authority (Wilayah) of the Messenger, for propagation and conveying the call of Islam to the ears of the world, for saving the oppressed, and for preventing corruption. Based on narrations, Allah's share is at the disposal of the Messenger, and the Messenger's share, and after him, the Imam's share. [528] And these three shares, during the occultation of the Imam, are placed at the disposal of His specific deputies or His general deputies, meaning the qualified Mujtahid and Marja' al-Taqlid. [529]

«ذى القربى» (the near kin), due to numerous Shi’a narrations [530] and also some Sunni narrations, does not refer to all relatives of the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله, but rather refers to the Imams of the Ahl al-Bayt علیهم السلام who hold the position of Imamate and leadership. Khums belongs to the Islamic leader and government, not the Prophet's family. In addition to the narrations, the placement of Dhil Qurba alongside «اللّه و للرسول», especially with the letter "Lam" (for), indicates that Dhil Qurba are specific individuals who are aligned with God and the Messenger.

Among the other expenditures of Khums mentioned in narrations are the poor (Masakin) and the wayfarers (Ibn al-Sabil) from the Sayyids of Bani Hashim, because it is forbidden for the poor Sayyids to receive Zakat. Thus, their needs must be met through Khums. [531] In reality, Islam has made two things obligatory to eliminate deprivation in society: one is Zakat, which relates to all the poor in society, and the other is Khums, a portion of which is specifically for the poor Sayyids. Khums and Zakat are paid to the poor up to the amount of their annual need, and no more. Imam Reza علیه السلام said: The decision regarding the amount of the share for each of the six categories mentioned in the Ayah rests with the Imam. [532]

524) An-Nisa, 94 525) Nahj al-Balaghah, Sermon 120 526) For further explanation, refer to the author's book on Khums 527) For further explanation, refer to Tawdih al-Masail (The Clarification of Issues) 528) Tafsir Safi 529) Tafsir Namuneh 530) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn and Al-Kafi, Vol. 1, p. 544 531) Tafsir Majma' al-Bayan, Wasa'il, Vol. 6, Book of Khums 532) Tafsir Safi

Messages

  1. Payment of Khums is obligatory, even if the income or spoils are small. «غنمتم من شى ء»

  2. Now that you have achieved victory in Badr with Divine help, pay the right of Allah, the Messenger, the Ahl al-Bayt علیهم السلام, and the Khums of the spoils. «غنمتم... فانّ للّه خُمُسه»

  3. Islam accepts the ownership of legal entities. «للرسول و لذى القربى»

  4. Eliminating deprivation is part of Islam's program, and the fruit of Jihad and spoils must be used for the benefit of the poor. «والمساكین و ابن السبیل»

  5. To honor the deprived class, their names are placed alongside the names of Allah, the Messenger, and the Ahl al-Bayt. «للّه خمسه و للرسول و لذى القربى و الیتامى والمساكین...»

  6. Faith is the factor for acting upon duty and sacrifice. «للّه خمسه ... ان كنتم آمنتم»

  7. Giving up wealth is so difficult that sometimes even fighters hesitate. «غنمتم... ان كنتم...»

  8. The sign of complete faith is paying Khums. Following the mention of faith, worship, migration, and Jihad, He says: «لِلّه خمسه... ان كنتم آمنتم...» Therefore, Khums is a permanent and continuous obligation, not seasonal or temporary, because it is a condition of faith.

  9. Servitude (to Allah) is the secret to receiving Divine knowledge, and the Holy Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله was a pure servant of God. «و ما انزلنا على عبدنا»

  10. The presence of the Prophet among the Muslims is the factor for God's help and their victory. «ما انزلنا على عبدنا یوم الفرقان»

  11. The day of battle is the day of separation between the truthful claimants and the liars. «یوم الفرقان یوم التقى الجمعان»

  12. Divine aid in the Battle of Badr clarified the truthfulness of Islam, «یوم الفرقان», and was a manifestation of Allah's power. «واللّه على كلّ شى ء قدیر»

Ayah

إِذْ أَنتُم بِٱلْعُدْوَةِ ٱلدُّنْيَا وَهُم بِٱلْعُدْوَةِ ٱلْقُصْوَىٰ وَٱلرَّكْبُ أَسْفَلَ مِنكُمْ ۚ وَلَوْ تَوَاعَدتُّمْ لَٱخْتَلَفْتُمْ فِى ٱلْمِيعَـٰدِ ۙ وَلَـٰكِن لِّيَقْضِىَ ٱللَّهُ أَمْرًا كَانَ مَفْعُولًا لِّيَهْلِكَ مَنْ هَلَكَ عَنۢ بَيِّنَةٍ وَيَحْيَىٰ مَنْ حَىَّ عَنۢ بَيِّنَةٍ ۗ وَإِنَّ ٱللَّهَ لَسَمِيعٌ عَلِيمٌ (٤۲)
When you were on the nearer side, and they on the farther side, while the caravan was below you, and had you agreed together on an encounter, you would have certainly failed to keep the tryst, but in order that Allah may carry through a matter that was bound to be fulfilled, so that he who perishes might perish by a manifest proof, and he who lives may live on by a manifest proof, and Allah is indeed all-hearing, all-knowing. (42)

Key Points

The word 'udwah (عُدوَة) is derived from 'adw (عَدو), meaning to transgress. It is also used for the margins and sides of things that have passed the middle limit. In this Ayah, 'udwah refers to the side and vicinity. The word dunya (دنیا) is derived from dunuw (دُنُوّ), meaning lower and closer, while quswa (قُصوى) and aqsa (اَقصى) mean farther away. In the Battle of Badr, the disbelievers were superior not only in terms of weaponry, numbers, and physical and spiritual readiness, but also in their strategic location. They could have found an escape route along the Red Sea coast. However, Allah brought the Muslims, who had only come intending to confiscate the disbelievers' property, face-to-face with them, leaving no choice but conflict. Only the grace of Allah led the Muslims to victory.

Portrait of the Battle of Badr The depiction of the Battle of Badr, from the beginning verses of the Surah until now, better illustrates the divine aids: 1. In the Battle of Badr, you were thinking of confiscating property and were not prepared for war. «تُوَدّون أن غیر ذات الشوكة تكون لكم» (Ayah 7) 2. When the war occurred, some of you were displeased. «فریقاً من المؤمنین لكارهون» (Ayah 5) 3. Some feared death. «كانّما یساقون الى الموت» (Ayah 6) 4. You were distressed and supplicating. «تستغیثون ربّكم» (Ayah 9) 5. We sent rain for purification from impurities and to solidify the sandy ground. (Ayah 11) 6. You did not have complete obedience to the command. «قالوا سمعنا و هم لایسمعون» (Ayah 21) 7. Some of you had previously committed treachery (like Abu Lubabah). (Ayah 27) 8. Your leader was exposed to threats and conspiracies. «یمكر بك» (Ayah 30) 9. If the matter had been left to your choice, you would not have reached an agreement despite all these problems. «لاختلفتم» And other examples, all of which show that the occurrence of the Battle of Badr was a divine plan to display the power of the Muslims and strike a blow to the morale of the disbelievers and polytheists. For this reason, in the previous Ayah, the day of the Battle of Badr was named the "Day of Furqan" (Criterion), because so much unseen aid descended that the truth became clear to everyone, and anyone who disregarded it, despite this clarity, did so out of enmity and obstinacy. «لیهلك مَن هلك عن بیّنة»

Messages

  1. Remembering past events, divine aids, and analyzing them is necessary. «اذ انتم...»

  2. Where Allah wills, He removes all factors of weakness. «لیقضى اللّه امراً كان مفعولا»

  3. The victory of the Muslims at Badr is a clear proof against polytheism and a manifest evidence for the truth of Monotheism and Islam. «لیقضى اللّه... لیهلك من هلك عن بیّنة ویحیى...»

  4. Conscious guidance and faith are valuable, just as conscious destruction and misguidance are extremely ugly. «لیهلك... عن بیّنة ویحیى... عن بیّنة»

  5. Islam is founded upon clear proofs and evidence, and such a school of thought is a source of life. «و یحیى من حىّ عن بیّنة»

  6. Allah had predetermined the victory of the Muslims, hence the scenes and decisions were altered. «لیقضى اللّه امراً كان مفعولا» From this, one can recognize Him as the One Who is All-Hearing and All-Knowing. «انّ اللّه لسمیع علیم»

Ayah

إِذْ يُرِيكَهُمُ ٱللَّهُ فِى مَنَامِكَ قَلِيلًا ۖ وَلَوْ أَرَىٰكَهُمْ كَثِيرًا لَّفَشِلْتُمْ وَلَتَنَـٰزَعْتُمْ فِى ٱلْأَمْرِ وَلَـٰكِنَّ ٱللَّهَ سَلَّمَ ۗ إِنَّهُۥ عَلِيمٌۢ بِذَاتِ ٱلصُّدُورِ (٤۳)
When Allah showed them to you as few in your dream, and had He shown them as many, you would have lost heart, and disputed about the matter. But Allah spared you. Indeed He knows well what is in the breasts. (43)

Key Points

Following the description of Allah's favors and aid to the Muslims, this Ayah speaks about making the disbelievers appear few in their eyes, which occurred in several stages:

First, the Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله saw them as few in a dream, and relating this dream to the Muslims boosted their morale.

Second, He made the Muslims appear few in the eyes of the disbelievers, so that they would not request fresh troops from Mecca for aid (which is discussed in the next Ayah).

Also, 'Atikah, the aunt of the Prophet, had a dream in Mecca three days before the Battle of Badr that someone was shouting: O people of Mecca! Hasten toward your place of slaughter! Then the caller, standing atop Mount Abu Qubays, shook a rock; the rock shattered, and a piece of it fell upon each of the houses, and blood flowed from Mecca. This dream was passed from mouth to mouth and struck terror into the disbelievers. These were Allah's plans for the victory of the Muslims in the Battle of Badr. [533]

The Noble Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله said: Dreams are of three types: either glad tidings from Allah, or grief and sorrow from Satan, or the daily problems of a person that he sees in his sleep. [534]

The dreams of the Prophets and the Divine Saints are of the first type, and thus they possess authority (are binding). In another hadith, He said: The vision (ru'ya) of righteous individuals is glad tidings from Allah and is a part of the components of prophethood. [535]

Of course, the dreams of the Divine Saints sometimes require interpretation, such as the dream of Prophet Yusuf (Joseph) عليه السلام regarding the sun, moon, and stars prostrating to him. And sometimes they are a statement of a ruling or an event and do not require interpretation, such as the dream of Prophet Ibrahim (Abraham) عليه السلام regarding the sacrifice of his son Isma'il (Ishmael), or the vision of the Prophet in this Ayah.

Although the dreams of the Prophets are a reflection of revelation, and the Qur'an repeatedly speaks of the truthful visions (ru'ya sadiqah) of Prophet Ibrahim عليه السلام and the Noble Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله, the fact that the Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله saw a large crowd as few in his dream indicates seeing the inner reality of the disbelievers, which is nothing but weakness and inability.

As He says elsewhere: «تحسبهم جمیعاً و قلوبهم شتّى» [536] You think they are united, but their hearts are far apart and scattered.

(533) Tafsir Namuneh (534) Bihar, Vol. 14, p. 441 (535) Bihar, Vol. 61, p. 177 (536) Hashr, 14

Messages

1- Sleep (dreams) is one of the means of communication, seeking help, boosting morale, and receiving Divine aid. «اذ یریكهم اللّه فى منامك»

2- The wondrous world of sleep and dreams has its own specific language and code. In the culture of dream interpretation and exegesis, the weakness and defeat of the enemy appear in the form of few numbers, even if their actual count is large. «فى منامك قلیلاً»

3- Disseminating information and statistics, stating the enemy's strengths, and anything that causes discord and weakens the morale of the Muslims, especially during war and on the front lines, is forbidden. «و لو أراكهم كثیراً لفشلتم...» (The morale of the fighters has a significant impact on victory and defeat.)

4- Allah protects the believers even in the most critical conditions. «ولكنّ اللّه سلّم»

If my Protector is the One Whom I know, He keeps the glass safe within the embrace of stone.

Ayah

وَإِذْ يُرِيكُمُوهُمْ إِذِ ٱلْتَقَيْتُمْ فِىٓ أَعْيُنِكُمْ قَلِيلًا وَيُقَلِّلُكُمْ فِىٓ أَعْيُنِهِمْ لِيَقْضِىَ ٱللَّهُ أَمْرًا كَانَ مَفْعُولًا ۗ وَإِلَى ٱللَّهِ تُرْجَعُ ٱلْأُمُورُ (٤٤)
And when He showed them to you as few in your eyes, when you met them [on the battlefield], and He made you [appear] few in their eyes, [it was] in order that Allah may carry through a matter that was bound to be fulfilled, and to Allah all matters are returned. (44)

Key Points

When the disbelievers saw the Muslims, they appeared so few in their eyes that they said: We will send our slaves to destroy the Muslims, [537] but when the battle started, during the heat of the fight, they saw the Muslims as numerous and double their number, and they were intimidated. [538] Just as Allah says elsewhere: «یرونهم مثلَیهم رأى العین» [539]

537) Tafsir Safi 538) Tafasir Namuneh and Al-Mizan 539) Aal-i 'Imran, 13

Messages

1- Divine decrees do not negate human free will. «واذ یریكموهم اذ التقیتم» The encouragement of the two armies at the Battle of Badr to confront each other indicates that the occurrence of the war was not outside their control.

2- Divine assistance encompassed the Muslims both before the war, «فى منامك», and during the war. «یریكموهم اذِ التَقَیتُم...»

3- Sometimes, in order to aid the Muslims, Allah intervenes in the sight (of people) by exercising His creative authority (Wilayah Takwini). «یریكموهم... فى أعینكم قلیلاً»

4- The utilization of the apparent senses is also in the hand of Allah. «فى اعینكم قلیلاً و یقلّلكم فى اعینهم»

5- If Allah wills the realization of a matter, He removes all obstacles. «لیقضى اللّه امراً...» (This phrase is repeated once in Ayah 44 and again in this Ayah.)

6- Victory is not solely dependent on population and numbers, but rather factors such as the faith and morale of the fighters, and the grace and decree of Allah are decisive. (The entire Ayah)

Ayah

يَـٰٓأَيُّهَا ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوٓا۟ إِذَا لَقِيتُمْ فِئَةً فَٱثْبُتُوا۟ وَٱذْكُرُوا۟ ٱللَّهَ كَثِيرًا لَّعَلَّكُمْ تُفْلِحُونَ (٤٥)
O you who have faith! When you meet a host [in battle], then stand firm, and remember Allah greatly so that you may be felicitous. (45)

Key Points

The meaning of the remembrance (dhikr) of Allah in the Ayah is not merely verbal remembrance, but rather internal attention, remembering the favors and aids of Allah, His promises, and remembering His might, greatness, and command are also intended. As Imam Sadiq عليه السلام said: Among the important matters that Allah has made obligatory upon His servants is remembering Allah frequently—Divine remembrance when encountering the lawful (halal) and the unlawful (haram)—so that if it is obedience to Allah, he acts upon it, and if it is disobedience to Allah, he abandons it. (540)

540) Bihar, vol. 93, p. 163

Messages

1- The prerequisite of faith is steadfastness. «یا ایّها الّذین آمنوا... فاثبتوا»

2- We are commanded to be steadfast on the battlefield, «فاثبتوا», but we must also ask Allah for this very perseverance. «ربّنا افرغ علینا صبراً و ثبّت اقدامنا» (541)

3- The atmosphere of the Muslim front must be filled with the remembrance of Allah, and the greater the difficulties, the greater the need for the remembrance of Allah. «واذكروا اللّه كثیراً»

4- A mere remembrance of Allah is not enough; we must remember Allah frequently. «واذكروا اللّه كثیراً» Because during the crisis of war and battlefield events, if the remembrance of Allah is not frequent, the possibility of deviation in goal, action, and method is greater.

5- On the battlefield, the remembrance of Allah must replace the remembrance of wives, children, wealth, etc. «واذكروا اللّه» As Imam Sajjad عليه السلام states in the Supplication for the Border Guards: O God! Remove the remembrance of wealth, children, and so forth from the hearts of the combatants, and make Paradise manifest before their eyes. (542)

6- Steadfastness and the remembrance of Allah are the secret to victory in battle and eternal salvation. «فاثبتوا واذكروا اللّه... لعلّكم تفلحون»

7- Battlefields and fronts are among the centers of salvation. «لعلّكم تفلحون»

541) Baqarah, 250

542) Sahifah Sajjadiyyah, Supplication 27

Ayah

وَأَطِيعُوا۟ ٱللَّهَ وَرَسُولَهُۥ وَلَا تَنَـٰزَعُوا۟ فَتَفْشَلُوا۟ وَتَذْهَبَ رِيحُكُمْ ۖ وَٱصْبِرُوٓا۟ ۚ إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ مَعَ ٱلصَّـٰبِرِينَ (٤٦)
And obey Allah and His Apostle, and do not dispute, or you will lose heart and your power will be gone. And be patient; indeed Allah is with the patient. (46)

Key Points

In the previous Ayah, two factors contributing to the victory of the believers—namely, "steadfastness" and "remembrance of Allah"—were mentioned. In this Ayah, the factors of obedience and unity are discussed. Just as the previous Ayah referred to physical and outward firmness, this Ayah mentions patience (or endurance) in the psychological and inner aspect. Rīḥ (ريح), meaning wind, is a metaphor for power and glory, like a wind that causes flags to flutter, signifying establishment, success, and greatness. If this greatness is lost due to disagreement and conflict, you will become humiliated and contemptible. Just as if the air leaves a car tire or a sports ball, many movements, journeys, and sports activities are halted. Unity, solidarity, and avoidance of disagreement and division are strict divine commands that Allah has ordered in numerous verses. This command must be observed in all areas, especially during war and conflict with the enemy, where the necessity is greater. Thus, Allah praised the unity, order, and coordination of the Mujāhidīn (striving ones) in Sūrat As-Saff: «انّ اللّه یحبّ الّذین یقاتلون فى سبیل اللّه صفّاً كانّهم بنیان مَرصوص» [543]

543) Saff, 6

Messages

1- The Jihād (struggle) of Muslims must be under the command of the leader of the Muslims and by the order of Allah and His Messenger (and their rightful successors). «اذا لقیتم فئة فاثبتوا... و اطیعوا اللّه و رسوله»

2- Divine law and the divine leader are the axis of unity. «اطیعوا اللّه و رسوله» (Aṭīʿū [Obey] refers to divine laws, and Rasūlih [His Messenger] refers to the heavenly leader.)

3- Conflict hollows you out internally and disgraces you externally. «فتفشلوا و تذهب ریحكم» (Throughout history, how many weaknesses and defeats have afflicted Muslims due to their failure to act upon this Ayah!)

4- We must be steadfast in obedience and unity, and if something is against our desire, or if action is taken against our desire, we must be patient and tolerate one another. «واصبروا»

5- Belief that Allah is with the patient drives a person toward patience and steadfastness. «واصبروا انّ اللّه مع الصابرین»

6- Divine help and assistance are for the patient. «ان اللّه مع الصابرین»

Ayah

وَلَا تَكُونُوا۟ كَٱلَّذِينَ خَرَجُوا۟ مِن دِيَـٰرِهِم بَطَرًا وَرِئَآءَ ٱلنَّاسِ وَيَصُدُّونَ عَن سَبِيلِ ٱللَّهِ ۚ وَٱللَّهُ بِمَا يَعْمَلُونَ مُحِيطٌ (٤۷)
Do not be like those who left their homes vainly and to show off to the people, and to bar [other people] from the way of Allah, and Allah comprehends what they do. (47)

Key Points

When Abu Sufyan's trade caravan safely reached Mecca, Abu Sufyan sent a message to Abu Jahl saying that they had arrived safely in Mecca, and those who had gone to help them should return. Abu Jahl arrogantly said: We will not return until we crush the Muslims, drink wine in celebration of victory, and demonstrate our power. However, despite all their arrogance and obstinacy, they were ultimately defeated, and Abu Jahl drank the wine of death. In this Ayah and the two preceding Ayahs, the factors for success on the battlefield are stated as follows: steadfastness, remembrance of Allah, obedience to leadership, avoidance of discord, patience, avoidance of arrogance, hypocrisy (Riya), and worldliness.

Messages

1- Arrogance (Batar) and hypocrisy (Riya) are among the scourges of power on the battlefield. «خرجوا من دیارهم بطراً ورئاء الناس»

2- The fundamental difference between Islamic and non-Islamic wars lies in the objective. The objective of Muslims in war is to remove discord/persecution (Ayah 39 of this Surah), but the objective of others is domination and display of power. «بطراً و رئاء النّاس»

3- When setting out for the battlefield, purify your intention and do not think of showing off. «رئاء النّاس» (Although the context of the Ayahs relates to the Battle of Badr, it also includes hypocrites who go to the front line hypocritically, for show-off, or to spread rumors and weaken morale.)

4- The front of falsehood strives to block the path of truth and prevent the spiritual and divine movement of society. «و یصدّون عن سبیل اللّه»

5- Let the enemies know that despite all the efforts they make against Allah, they will never escape from under His divine power. «واللّه بما یعملون محیط»

Ayah

وَإِذْ زَيَّنَ لَهُمُ ٱلشَّيْطَـٰنُ أَعْمَـٰلَهُمْ وَقَالَ لَا غَالِبَ لَكُمُ ٱلْيَوْمَ مِنَ ٱلنَّاسِ وَإِنِّى جَارٌ لَّكُمْ ۖ فَلَمَّا تَرَآءَتِ ٱلْفِئَتَانِ نَكَصَ عَلَىٰ عَقِبَيْهِ وَقَالَ إِنِّى بَرِىٓءٌ مِّنكُمْ إِنِّىٓ أَرَىٰ مَا لَا تَرَوْنَ إِنِّىٓ أَخَافُ ٱللَّهَ ۚ وَٱللَّهُ شَدِيدُ ٱلْعِقَابِ (٤۸)
When Satan made their deeds seem decorous to them, and said [to the faithless], ‘No one shall overcome you today from among all mankind, and I will stand by you.’ But when the two hosts sighted each other, he took to his heels, saying, ‘Indeed I am quit of you. I see what you do not see. Indeed I fear Allah, and Allah is severe in retribution.’ (48)

Key Points

The word jār (جار) means helper, partner, neighbor, and ally (one who shares an oath). The first meaning is more appropriate here. The word nakas (نَكَص) means refusal and retreat, and ʿaqib (عَقِب) means the heel of the foot, and nakasa ʿalā ʿaqibayh (نَكَص على عقبیه) means he turned on his heels and retreated. The dialogue between Satan and his followers mentioned in this Ayah might be the satanic whispers (waswasah), or it might be that Satan pursues his goals in the guise of a human, engaging in temptation and seduction in the appearance of a person, concerning which narrations have also been reported. (544) Just as Satan, during the Battle of Badr, appeared in the form of Surāqah ibn Mālik (one of the prominent figures of the Banū Kinānah tribe) and encouraged and incited the polytheists, promising them support and victory. However, with the presence of the angels, on the verge of the Islamic army's victory, he took flight. After the defeat of the disbelievers, they told him in Mecca, "You caused our defeat!" And he swore that he had not even left Mecca. Thus, it became clear that Satan, in the guise of Surāqah, had promised them victory and then fled. Satan carries out propaganda work for the side of falsehood and gives them morale at the beginning of the conflict, but at the stage of confrontation, upon seeing the Divine aids, he abandons them and retreats.

544) Tafsīr Majma‘ al-Bayān and Nūr al-Thaqalayn

Messages

1- One of Satan's methods of deception is making evil appear beautiful. Zayyana (زیّن). Indeed, considering bad deeds and abnormalities as good is a sign of Satan's influence on human thoughts.

2- Satan and the satanic incite and justify others toward corruption. Zayyana, lā ghālib, jār lakum.

3- Faith attracts the support of angels, and disbelief attracts the support of Satan. Jār lakum.

4- Satan is a hypocrite, a deceiver, a liar, and disloyal. Jār lakum... barī’un minkum.

5- Satans ignite the fire of discord (fitnah), but they do not place themselves in the battle. Innī barī’un minkum.

6- Reliance on other than Allah causes a person to be abandoned in times of difficulty. Innī jār lakum... Innī barī’un minkum.

7- Non-Divine relationships are unstable and unreliable. Qāla innī barī’un minkum.

8- Satan is aware of the power of the angels. Innī arā mā lā tarawn. And this observation of the angels' power caused his retreat. Qāla innī barī’un minkum.

9- Satan is capable of observing certain hidden matters. Innī arā mā lā tarawn.

10- Satan believes in Allah and is aware of the severity of His punishment. Innī akhāfu Allāha wa Allāhu shadīdu al-ʿiqāb. Although, perhaps, according to Ibn ʿAbbās, Satan falsely claimed fear of Allah, otherwise his situation would not have reached this point. (545)

545) Tafsīr Ithnā ʿAsharī

Ayah

إِذْ يَقُولُ ٱلْمُنَـٰفِقُونَ وَٱلَّذِينَ فِى قُلُوبِهِم مَّرَضٌ غَرَّ هَـٰٓؤُلَآءِ دِينُهُمْ ۗ وَمَن يَتَوَكَّلْ عَلَى ٱللَّهِ فَإِنَّ ٱللَّهَ عَزِيزٌ حَكِيمٌ (٤۹)
When the hypocrites said, and [also] those in whose hearts is a sickness, ‘Their religion has deceived them.’ But whoever puts his trust in Allah, then Allah is indeed all-mighty, all-wise. (49)

Key Points

The hypocrites (munafiqūn) and those weak in faith, who did not believe in divine help (Nasr), did not imagine that the Muslims, with their small numbers and few weapons, would be victorious, and they considered them deceived. It is possible that the hypocrites referred to are some Muslims in Medina, and that "و الّذین فى قلوبهم مرض" (those in whose hearts is a disease) refers to the weak-in-faith individuals in Mecca. [546] And it is possible that the hypocrites are those who outwardly claimed Islam in Medina, or those who became Muslim in Mecca but refused to support the Prophet and migrate with the Muslims.

546) Tafsir Kabir of Fakhr Razi

Messages

1- Those who have an unhealthy spirit become aligned with the hypocrites. «اذ یقول المنافقون والّذین فى قلوبهم مرض»

2- The hypocrites consider the Muslims deceived and arrogant. (And they say: Can one go to war against weapons merely with "Allahu Akbar" and empty hands?! Is it possible...) «غرّ هؤلاء دینهم»

3- Inclinations and viewpoints influence how a person interprets things. One action can lead to several interpretations; one person considers it reliance (tawakkul), and another considers it deception and arrogance. «اذ یقول المنافقون و الّذین فى قلوبهم مرض غرّ هؤلاء... و من یتوكّل»

4- Reliance upon Allah is separate from arrogance. «ومن یتوكّل...» (Reliance upon God during the Battle of Badr was the factor leading to victory.)

5- One must rely upon and trust a Power that is invincible and whose every action is wise. «عزیز حكیم» (The power of Allah is accompanied by might and wisdom.)

Ayahs

وَلَوْ تَرَىٰٓ إِذْ يَتَوَفَّى ٱلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا۟ ۙ ٱلْمَلَـٰٓئِكَةُ يَضْرِبُونَ وُجُوهَهُمْ وَأَدْبَـٰرَهُمْ وَذُوقُوا۟ عَذَابَ ٱلْحَرِيقِ (٥٠)
Were you to see when the angels take away the faithless, striking their faces and their backs, [saying], ‘Taste the punishment of the burning. (50)
ذَٰلِكَ بِمَا قَدَّمَتْ أَيْدِيكُمْ وَأَنَّ ٱللَّهَ لَيْسَ بِظَلَّـٰمٍ لِّلْعَبِيدِ (٥۱)
That is because of what your hands have sent ahead, and because Allah is not tyrannical to the servants.’ (51)

Key Points

The Quran repeatedly mentions the difficult passing (death) of the disbelievers, including in Surah Muhammad, Ayah 27, and Surah Al-An'am, Ayah 93. Conversely, the easy passing of the believers is also mentioned, such as in Ayah 32 of Surah An-Nahl, where we read: «الّذین یتوفّاكم الملائكة طیّبین یقولون سلام علیكم ادخلوا الجنّة بما كنتم تعملون»

«یتوفّى» (Yatawaffa) means "to take" or "to seize." The Quran states: We take the disbelievers, and it does not say: We take his soul (Ruh). However, since the human body remains in this world, it becomes clear that it is his soul that is taken. Therefore, the reality of man lies in his soul. As long as a person is alive, the soul is accompanied by his body, and after death, it separates from his body and remains (persists).

Messages

1- By describing His wrath towards the disbelievers, Allah consoles the Prophet and the believers. «و لوترى...»

2- The divine angels recognize the believers and the disbelievers. «اذ یتوفّى الّذین كفروا»

3- Allah's wrath in the Hereafter towards the disbelievers begins from the moment of death. «اذ یتوفّى... یضربون وجوههم...»

4- There is also punishment in the Barzakh (intermediate realm). «ذوقوا عذاب الحریق»

5- Divine punishments are the consequence of man's own deeds, not Allah's vengeance. «ذلك بما قدّمت ایدیكم...»

6- Oppression against one person is equivalent to oppression against all. «لیس بظلاّم للعبید» (The slightest injustice from Allah would be an extremely great oppression, and He is far removed from that.)

7- Divine punishments are based on justice. «لیس بظلاّم للعبید»

Ayah

كَدَأْبِ ءَالِ فِرْعَوْنَ ۙ وَٱلَّذِينَ مِن قَبْلِهِمْ ۚ كَفَرُوا۟ بِـَٔايَـٰتِ ٱللَّهِ فَأَخَذَهُمُ ٱللَّهُ بِذُنُوبِهِمْ ۗ إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ قَوِىٌّ شَدِيدُ ٱلْعِقَابِ (٥۲)
Like the precedent of Pharaoh’s clan and those who were before them, who defied Allah’s signs, so Allah seized them for their sins. Indeed Allah is all-strong, severe in retribution. (52)

Messages

1- Sometimes disbelief (kufr) and obstinacy become a habit and characteristic for some people, and in this case, punishment descends. «كدأب»

2- All nations are equal before Allah, and if they follow the path of deviation and disbelief, they will be punished. «كدأب آل فرعون والّذین من قبلهم كفروا... فاخذهم اللّه» Yes, the Divine tradition (Sunnah) regarding the punishment of disbelievers is fixed, and the retribution of the polytheists in the Battle of Badr was not a special ruling.

3- Sin is the cause of Divine wrath. «فاخذهم اللّه بذنوبهم»

4- Allah is not powerless in punishing the disbelievers, and all nations (such as the powerful Pharaoh or the weak polytheists) are helpless before the wrath of Allah. «انّ اللّه قوى شدید العقاب»

Ayah

ذَٰلِكَ بِأَنَّ ٱللَّهَ لَمْ يَكُ مُغَيِّرًا نِّعْمَةً أَنْعَمَهَا عَلَىٰ قَوْمٍ حَتَّىٰ يُغَيِّرُوا۟ مَا بِأَنفُسِهِمْ ۙ وَأَنَّ ٱللَّهَ سَمِيعٌ عَلِيمٌ (٥۳)
That is because Allah never changes a blessing that He has bestowed on a people unless they change what is in their own souls, and Allah is all-hearing, all-knowing: (53)

Key Points

In numerous narrations (Ahadith), factors such as oppression and sin have been considered the cause of the change (removal) of Divine blessings, just as returning from sin and deviation and moving on the path of truth leads to the pouring forth of all types of Divine blessings. [547] Sins and injustices distance man from the worthiness of benefiting from Divine grace. As Imam Ali عليه السلام pointed out in Khutbah al-Qasi'ah (the Sermon of the Scorned), and as we read in Du'a Kumayl: «الّلهم اغفر لى الذنوب الّتى تهتك العصم... الّلهم اغفر لى الذّنوب الّتى تغیّر النّعم...» In the letter of Imam Ali عليه السلام to Malik al-Ashtar, we read: Nothing changes Divine blessings like oppression and injustice, because Allah hears the cry of the oppressed and is lying in wait for the oppressors. [548] Imam Sadiq عليه السلام said: Always seek refuge in God from the hardships and afflictions of day and night, which are the punishment for your disobedience and sins. [549]

547) Tafasir Nur al-Thaqalayn and Furqan 548) Nahj al-Balaghah, Letter 53 549) Tafsir Ithna Ashari, Kafi, Vol. 2, p. 269

Messages

1- Man is inherently worthy of receiving Divine blessings. «انعمها على قوم»

2- The removal of blessings originates from ourselves; otherwise, God's tradition (Sunnah) is based on the continuity of blessings. «ذلك بانّ اللّه لم یك مغیّراً نعمة... حتّى یغیّروا ما بانفسهم»

3- Giving and taking by God is systematic and wise. «لم یك مغیّراً نعمة... حتّى یغیّروا...»

4- The Mercy of Allah precedes His Wrath. «انعمها على قوم حتّى یغیّروا»

5- In Islam, besides individual punishment, social punishments are also legislated. «على قوم»

6- Human nature (Fitrah) is founded upon faith and purity, and it is man who changes himself. «یغیّروا ما بانفسهم»

7- Man makes history, not that economics, historical determinism, and environment make man. «حتّى یغیّروا...» Of course, environment and economics are not without effect, but the main role belongs to human will, and none of them compels man.

8- Individuals build society, and any positive or negative transformation in society is based on the intellectual and cultural transformation of individuals. «قوم... یغیّروا ما بانفسهم»

9- The prosperity and wretchedness of nations depend on their internal transformations, not on power and wealth. «حتّى یغیّروا ما بانفسهم»

10- In analyzing the destiny of societies, the axis is the spirit (morale) of the people, not luck, superstitions, ruling systems, historical determinism, etc. «یغیّروا ما بانفسهم»

11- All of us are under the observation of God, and the punishment of the disbelievers relies on the extensive knowledge of Allah. «انّ اللّه سمیع علیم»

Ayah

كَدَأْبِ ءَالِ فِرْعَوْنَ ۙ وَٱلَّذِينَ مِن قَبْلِهِمْ ۚ كَذَّبُوا۟ بِـَٔايَـٰتِ رَبِّهِمْ فَأَهْلَكْنَـٰهُم بِذُنُوبِهِمْ وَأَغْرَقْنَآ ءَالَ فِرْعَوْنَ ۚ وَكُلٌّ كَانُوا۟ ظَـٰلِمِينَ (٥٤)
Like the precedent of Pharaoh’s clan and those who were before them, who denied the signs of their Lord; so We destroyed them for their sins, and We drowned Pharaoh’s clan; and they were all wrongdoers. (54)

Key Points

The expression «كدأب آل فرعون» (like the custom of the people of Pharaoh) had also appeared in the two previous verses, but this repetition does not contradict eloquence, because the contexts are different. There, the discussion was about divine retribution against the oppressor; here, it is about the change of blessings based on the change in people's conditions. Of course, it is possible that in the previous verse, the reference was to the punishment of the Hereafter, and here, to the worldly punishment.

Messages

1- We must learn lessons from the past and the destiny of those who came before. «كدأب آل فرعون»

2- Denying the divine signs (Ayat) is the precursor to human destruction. «كذّبوا بآیات ربّهم فأهلكناهم»

3- Man possesses free will, and his destiny depends on his own actions. «كذّبوا بآیات ربّهم فأهلكناهم بذنوبهم»

4- Oppression (zulm) and sin are the cause of divine wrath, whether it is oppression against oneself, against people, or against the Prophets and the faith (Maktab). «أهلكناهم بذنوبهم... كانوا ظالمین»

5- Hardships, worldly incidents and events, and the disappearance of blessings are the consequences of human sins. «فأهلكناهم بذنوبهم و اغرقنا آل فرعون»

6- Denying the divine signs and sin constitute oppression (zulm). «كذّبوا... كانوا ظالمین»

Ayah

إِنَّ شَرَّ ٱلدَّوَآبِّ عِندَ ٱللَّهِ ٱلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا۟ فَهُمْ لَا يُؤْمِنُونَ (٥٥)
Indeed the worst of beasts in Allah’s sight are those who are faithless; so they will not have faith. (55)

Key Points

Ayah 22 of this Surah considered "شرّالدّوابّ" (the worst of creatures) to be those who do not reflect. This Ayah considers them to be those who do not believe and commit disbelief. Thus, the root of disbelief is the lack of correct reasoning. The Quran considers the value of man dependent on intellect and faith, such that if he does not reason or commits disbelief, he is outside the sphere of humanity. The true human is one who is rational and a believer. Indeed, a disbeliever who understood the truth but does not believe might hold a title among people, but in the sight of Allah, he is the worst creature.

Messages

1- Those who hear the call of the prophets but remain indifferent are the worst creatures. «انّ شرّ الدّوابّ... الّذین كفروا»

2- Disbelief causes man to fall to the low ranks of animality. «شرّ الدّوابّ... الّذین كفروا»

3- Sometimes disbelief becomes an ingrained trait (malakah) for man and darkens his future as well. «الّذین كفروا فهم لایؤمنون»

Ayah

ٱلَّذِينَ عَـٰهَدتَّ مِنْهُمْ ثُمَّ يَنقُضُونَ عَهْدَهُمْ فِى كُلِّ مَرَّةٍ وَهُمْ لَا يَتَّقُونَ (٥٦)
—Those with whom you made a treaty, and who violated their treaty every time, and who are not Godwary. (56)

Key Points

The covenant of the Noble Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله with the Jews was that they should not help the polytheists and should not seek to harm the Muslims. However, the Jews broke the covenant and, during the Battle of the Trench (Khandaq), sold weapons to the polytheists and aided them. It is mentioned in narrations: Whoever is not loyal and breaks a covenant is a hypocrite (munafiq), even if he performs prayer (salat) and observes fasting (sawm). [550]

550) Kafi, Vol. 2, p. 290

Messages

1- The leader of the Muslims can enter into a covenant with the disbelievers (kuffar). «عاهدت منهم» (You made a covenant with them), just as the Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله made numerous covenants with the disbelievers, even though they were not committed (to their word). «ینقضون عهدهم فى كلّ مرّة» (They break their covenant every time).

2- Covenant-breaking is not a reason for not making a covenant again. «ینقضون عهدهم فى كلّ مرّة» (They break their covenant every time).

3- Covenant-breaking is incompatible with humanity. «شرّالدّوابّ... ینقضون عهدهم» (The worst of creatures... they break their covenant).
