4- Disbelief (kufr) is the basis for covenant-breaking. «الّذین كفروا... ینقضون عهدهم» (Those who disbelieved... they break their covenant).

5- Covenant-breaking is a lack of piety (taqwa). «ینقضون عهدهم... و هم لایتّقون» (They break their covenant... and they are not pious).

6- The greater danger comes from those for whom covenant-breaking has become their method and characteristic, and who have no fear (of the consequences). «ینقضون... فى كل مرّة... لایتّقون» (They break... every time... they are not pious).

Ayah

فَإِمَّا تَثْقَفَنَّهُمْ فِى ٱلْحَرْبِ فَشَرِّدْ بِهِم مَّنْ خَلْفَهُمْ لَعَلَّهُمْ يَذَّكَّرُونَ (٥۷)
So if you confront them in battle, treat them [in such a wise] as to disperse those who are behind them, so that they may take admonition. (57)

Key Points

«تَثقَفنّهم» (tathqafannahum) is derived from «ثقف» (thaqf), meaning the comprehension of something with precision and speed. This means that when confronting the disbelievers (kuffār), you must be completely vigilant so that you are not taken by surprise. «تشرید» (tashrīd) means creating insecurity, anxiety, and scattering (dispersing). This means you should attack the enemy and plan your warfare in such a way that their hidden supporters and forces behind the front lines become terrified and do not contemplate attack or support.

Messages

1- Your confrontation with the enemies must be surprising, swift, precise, and crushing; this is one of the principles of warfare. «تثقفنّهم... فشرّد بهم»

2- The awareness and vision of the leader of the Muslims must be such that He recognizes the scope and continuation of the conspiracy and the conspirators, and takes a correct and decisive decision against them. «فشرّد بهم»

3- We should not be content with routine, first-line defenses, and we must not be oblivious to the enemy's rear forces and the planners behind the scenes. «مَن خلفهم» Just as in driving one should not only stare straight ahead, but must also look further and beyond.

4- The punishment for covenant-breakers who disrupt the security and stability of society is that we should make them terrified and anxious. «فشرّد بهم»

5- Although Islam is a religion of compassion and mercy, it does not tolerate treachery, covenant-breaking, or the disruption of order and security. «فشرّد بهم»

6- The Islamic society must possess awe and majesty. «فشرّد بهم من خلفهم»

7- We must strike fear into the disbelievers (kuffār) so that they abandon the thought of attacking us again. «فشرّد بهم»

8- Alongside propagation and admonition, Islam is also a religion of exercising power and confronting covenant-breakers and disruptors. «فشرّد بهم» And the front line of the enemy must be dealt with in such a way that it serves as a lesson for the subsequent ranks. «من خلفهم لعلّهم یذّكّرون»

Ayahs

وَإِمَّا تَخَافَنَّ مِن قَوْمٍ خِيَانَةً فَٱنۢبِذْ إِلَيْهِمْ عَلَىٰ سَوَآءٍ ۚ إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ لَا يُحِبُّ ٱلْخَآئِنِينَ (٥۸)
And if you fear treachery from a people, break off [the treaty] with them in a like manner. Indeed Allah does not like the treacherous. (58)
وَلَا يَحْسَبَنَّ ٱلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا۟ سَبَقُوٓا۟ ۚ إِنَّهُمْ لَا يُعْجِزُونَ (٥۹)
Let the faithless not suppose that they have outmanoeuvred [Allah]. Indeed, they cannot frustrate [His power]. (59)

Key Points

The word «فأنبذ الیهم» is derived from «نَبذ», meaning "to cast away." The intent of casting the covenant toward the enemy is that you must inform them beforehand and then cancel the covenant, so that they are not taken by surprise and you do not act dishonorably. «على سواء» either means reciprocity, meaning that just as they are plotting treachery and breaking the covenant, you should also cancel the covenant. Or it means dealing justly with the enemy. [551] The Ayah refers to a situation where there is evidence and indication of the enemy's plotting and treachery, necessitating preemptive action in canceling the covenant for prevention.

551) Tafsir-e Nemooneh and Al-Mizan

Messages

  1. In important military and social matters, do not wait for treachery to occur; rather, action must be taken even based on the probability of treachery. «و امّا تخافنّ... خیانة»
  2. Islam adheres to its contracts and treaties, and faithfulness is required unless there is fear of treachery. «و امّا تخافنّ... خیانة فأنبذ الیهم»
  3. The discernment of evidence and signs of treachery, and the decision to annul contracts and invalidate commitments, falls under the authority of the leader of the Islamic society. «فأنبذ الیهم» (The addressee is the Prophet personally, not the Islamic community.)
  4. Adherence to treaties lasts only as long as there is no fear of plotting; otherwise, withdrawal must be announced. «فأنبذ الیهم»
  5. Even if you fear the enemy's treachery, do not act dishonorably; announce the cancellation of the covenant. «فانبذ الیهم»
  6. Maintain justice and fairness even with the enemy. «على سواء» means "with justice" (بالعدل). [552]
  7. Canceling a contract based on reciprocity is just. «على سواء»
  8. Treachery, regardless of who commits it—whether a disbeliever or a Muslim—is forbidden (Haram) and ugly. «انّ اللّه لایحبّ الخائنین»
  9. Waging war without prior announcement against those with whom we have a history of covenant is treachery. «فأنبذ الیهم... انّ اللّه لا یحبّ الخائنین»
  10. One cannot outpace the divine traditions (Sunan). «یا یحسبنّ... سبقوا»
  11. Take the initiative in such a way that the disbelievers do not imagine they have outpaced you. «لایحسبنّ الّذین كفروا سبقوا»
  12. Disbelievers will not succeed through treachery. «لایعجزون»

552) Tafsir Al-Mizan

Ayah

وَأَعِدُّوا۟ لَهُم مَّا ٱسْتَطَعْتُم مِّن قُوَّةٍ وَمِن رِّبَاطِ ٱلْخَيْلِ تُرْهِبُونَ بِهِۦ عَدُوَّ ٱللَّهِ وَعَدُوَّكُمْ وَءَاخَرِينَ مِن دُونِهِمْ لَا تَعْلَمُونَهُمُ ٱللَّهُ يَعْلَمُهُمْ ۚ وَمَا تُنفِقُوا۟ مِن شَىْءٍ فِى سَبِيلِ ٱللَّهِ يُوَفَّ إِلَيْكُمْ وَأَنتُمْ لَا تُظْلَمُونَ (٦٠)
Prepare against them whatever you can of [military] power and war-horses, awing thereby the enemy of Allah, and your enemy, and others besides them, whom you do not know, but Allah knows them. And whatever you spend in the way of Allah will be repaid to you in full, and you will not be wronged. (60)

Key Points

| This Ayah commands comprehensive readiness (state of alert) for Muslims against enemies, requiring the preparation of every type of weapon, facility, means, and propaganda method, even slogans and anthems. Observing these commands causes the disbelievers (kuffar) to fear the combat strength of the Muslims. When the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله learned that a new weapon had been manufactured in Yemen, He sent a person to Yemen to acquire it. It is narrated from Him that with one arrow, three people enter Paradise: its maker, the one who prepares it, and the archer. [553] In Islam, archery and horseback riding competitions, and betting on them, are considered permissible in order to create combat readiness. The pronoun "لهم" (for them) refers back to the disbelievers (kuffar) and those mentioned in the previous Ayah whose treachery was feared. Meaning: be prepared against the enemy, whose potential for treachery always exists, even if a covenant or treaty is in place.

553) Tafsir Furqan

Messages

1- Muslims must possess complete combat readiness in terms of equipment, personnel, etc., and be prepared in advance to counter the enemy's treachery and attack. «و أعدّوا لهم ما استطعتم»

2- The goal of military readiness is the preservation of the Muslims' faith and homeland, not plunder, colonialism, or similar aims. «واعدّوا لهم...»

3- The government must allocate its maximum capacity in providing budget for the defense of the Islamic system and for intimidating the enemies of Allah and themselves. «مااستطعتم»

4- All Muslims must be ready to confront the enemy's plots and must have undergone combat training. [554] «مااستطعتم»

5- Dialogue and negotiation are not always effective or sufficient; sometimes power and strength must enter the field. «من قوّة»

6- At all times, prepare the most advanced combat facilities for defense and spare nothing, whether it be political and military power, or propaganda and logistical means. «من قوّة»

7- Instilling terror in the enemy's heart by any means, even through outward appearance, is necessary. «من قوّة» A Hadith states: Frighten the enemy by dyeing facial hair, so they do not say the army of Islam is old. [555]

8- For the purpose of irhab (terrorizing) and frightening the enemies, even military training for women is effective. «من قوّة... ترهبون به عدوّ اللّه»

9- Do not be satisfied with today's facilities, and expand military and defensive equipment to the limit of the enemies' threat. «من قوّة... ترهبون به عدوّ اللّه»

10- Unity and solidarity are also a type of power that must be secured, because with division, the enemy cannot be frightened. «من قوّة... ترهبون به عدوّ اللّه»

11- In Islam, all Muslims are soldiers, and popular mobilization (Basij) is necessary. «أعدّوا، ترهبون، تنفقوا و ...»

12- Warhorses, in those days, and military tools today, must be maintained and fed in a state of readiness. «رباط الخیل»

13- Both the readiness of forces and equipment are necessary. «من قوّة و من رباط الخیل»

14- Islam must have a system and a government, because without it, enemies have no fear. «ترهبون به»

15- The defensive structure of Muslims in cultural, economic, political, military, ideological, and other fields must be so strong that the enemy fears them from every direction and their plots are neutralized. («ترهبون به» is stated absolutely.)

16- Both Divine values and human rights must be preserved. «عدوّاللّه و عدوّكم»

17- In Islam, the faith and human dignity are paramount, not personal, racial, or tribal motives. «عدوّاللّه و عدوّكم»

18- Not all enemies are known. «لاتعلمونهم» (you do not know them). Perhaps the hypocrites (munafiqeen) are meant, as the same expression is used regarding them: «لاتعلمهم نحن نعلمهم» (You do not know them; We know them). [556]

19- Do not plan only for known enemies, but also have the necessary foresight even for unknown enemies of the future. «لاتعلمونهم»

20- Securing defensive power requires a budget; preaching and slogans are not enough. «تنفقوا»

21- The people must participate in the war budget and in supplying the front, and this is not limited only to wartime. «وما تنفقوا...» (The Ayah is absolute and indicates that Muslims must always spend money for readiness.)

22- Weapons and personnel alone are not enough; financial support is also necessary. «وما تنفقوا»

23- Helping the front is not only financial aid; help through every means—wealth, life, honor, information, the pen, etc.—is necessary. «من شى ء»

24- Any effort, spending, and utilization of resources to strengthen the military structure of the Islamic government is an instance of spending in the way of Allah. «وما تنفقوا من شى ء فى سبیل اللّه»

25- The goodness and blessing of spending will return to you only on the condition that it is in the way of Allah, not out of hypocrisy (riya), seeking reputation (sum'ah), showing off, or based on relationships and considerations. «فى سبیل اللّه»

26- Financial aid leads to the power of the Ummah, and its result returns to the people themselves, the economic situation of the society, and the dignity of the Muslims. «یوفّ الیكم»

27- Violating the rights of others and not paying wages and rewards for work completely is oppression (zulm). «یوفّ الیكم و انتم لا تظلمون»

554) Imam Khomeini (may Allah have mercy on him) said during the early days of the victory of the Islamic Revolution, when the population of Iran was about thirty million: We must have a twenty-million-man army.

555) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn

556) Tawbah, 101

Ayah

۞ وَإِن جَنَحُوا۟ لِلسَّلْمِ فَٱجْنَحْ لَهَا وَتَوَكَّلْ عَلَى ٱللَّهِ ۚ إِنَّهُۥ هُوَ ٱلسَّمِيعُ ٱلْعَلِيمُ (٦۱)
If they incline toward peace, then you [too] incline toward it, and put your trust in Allah. Indeed He is the All-hearing, the All-knowing. (61)

Key Points

Imam Ali (عليه السلام) wrote to Malik al-Ashtar: «لاتدفعنّ صُلحاً دعاك الیه عَدوّك و لكن اَلحذَر اَلحذَر من عدوّك بعد صُلحِه، فان العدوّ ربّما قارب لیتغفّل» [557] Do not reject the peace offered by your enemy, but after the peace, be vigilant and beware of the enemy's deceit, because sometimes the enemy's proximity is for the purpose of catching you unawares.

557) Nahj al-Balaghah, Letter 53

Messages

1- Muslims must be at a level of power such that the request for peace comes from the enemy. «و ان جنحوا»

2- Islam is not warmongering. «وان جنحوا... فاجنح»

3- The command for war or the acceptance of peace rests with the Prophet of Allah and is among the prerogatives of the leadership of the Islamic society. «فاجنح» (He) did not say: «فاجنحوا»

4- Do not abuse your position of power. «فاجنح»

5- When accepting the peace proposal, rely upon Allah. «فاجنح لها و توكّل»

6- In accepting peace, there is both the possibility of danger and conspiracy, as well as the harsh criticism of some friends, but one must rely upon Allah. «توكّل على اللّه»

7- Belief in the absolute hearing and comprehensive knowledge of Allah is the foundation for man's reliance upon Him. «توكّل على اللّه انّه هو السمیع العلیم»

Ayah

وَإِن يُرِيدُوٓا۟ أَن يَخْدَعُوكَ فَإِنَّ حَسْبَكَ ٱللَّهُ ۚ هُوَ ٱلَّذِىٓ أَيَّدَكَ بِنَصْرِهِۦ وَبِٱلْمُؤْمِنِينَ (٦۲)
But if they desire to deceive you, Allah is indeed sufficient for you. It is He who strengthened you with His help and with the means of the faithful, (62)

Key Points

This Ayah must be considered alongside Ayah 58, which states that if the leader of the Muslims, based on evidence and indications, is certain of the enemy's deceit under the guise of peace, the situation is different. However, if there is a possibility of good intention, he must accept it with vigilance. In some narrations, Divine help (نصرت الهى) has been interpreted as the support of Hadrat Ali عليه السلام for the Prophet. In the book of Ibn Asakir, it is narrated from Abu Hurayrah that the Noble Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله said: It is written upon the Divine Throne: «لا اله الاّ أنا وحدى لا شریك لى، محمّد عبدى و رسولى، ایّدته بعلىّ» (There is no god but Me, I am One, I have no partner, and Muhammad is My servant and Messenger, I have supported him through Ali عليه السلام). And this is the very word of God, Who said: «هو الّذى ایّدك بنصره و بالمؤمنین» (It is He Who supported you with His help and with the believers), so the help (النّصر) was Ali. [558]

558) Tafsir Durr al-Manthur, Vol. 3, p. 199, Mulhaqat Ihqaq al-Haqq, Vol. 3, p. 194 and Bihar, Vol. 27, p. 2

Messages

1- The leader of the Islamic society must accept the enemy's peace proposal so that Muslims are not portrayed as warmongers, but he must remain vigilant so as not to be deceived. «ان جنحوا... فاجنح... و ان یریدوا ان یخدعوك» (meaning the possibility of deceit exists).

2- If the people are ready and Allah's grace is included, all the enemy's conspiracies will be neutralized. «فانّ حسبك اللّه»

3- If we fulfill our duty, we should not fear difficulties, because Allah is the solver of problems. «أیّدك بنصره»

4- Divine assistance and popular support are by the will of Allah for the purpose of helping you. «ایّدك بنصره و بالمؤمنین»

5- The support of the people is effective through the will and desire of Allah. «فانّ حسبك اللّه هو الّذى ایّدك... بالمؤمنین»

6- Seeking help from the people as a means of Divine assistance, without neglecting Allah, is not polytheism (Shirk). «ایّدك بنصره و بالمؤمنین»

7- The people are the arm and support of the leader. «ایّدك... بالمؤمنین»

Ayah

وَأَلَّفَ بَيْنَ قُلُوبِهِمْ ۚ لَوْ أَنفَقْتَ مَا فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ جَمِيعًا مَّآ أَلَّفْتَ بَيْنَ قُلُوبِهِمْ وَلَـٰكِنَّ ٱللَّهَ أَلَّفَ بَيْنَهُمْ ۚ إِنَّهُۥ عَزِيزٌ حَكِيمٌ (٦۳)
and united their hearts. Had you spent all that is in the earth, you could not have united their hearts, but Allah united them together. Indeed He is all-mighty, all-wise. (63)

Key Points

| Question: In this Ayah, He states that even if you had spent all the wealth of the earth, you could not have created affection (or unity). Why then does Ayah 60 of Surah At-Tawbah state: «والمؤلّفة قلوبهم» (those whose hearts are to be reconciled), implying that a portion of Zakat must be spent on reconciling hearts (Ta'lif al-Qulub)? Answer: The degree, depth, and history of grudges among people vary. Sometimes a grudge is removed with a smile and a gift, but sometimes it is so deep that it cannot be erased by any means. Furthermore, the reconciliation of hearts (Ta'lif al-Qulub) is of three types: A: Affection between believers and believers, B: Affection between believers and disbelievers, C: Affection between disbelievers and disbelievers. However, what is impossible is the true affection of disbelievers with one another, but the affection of believers among themselves and the attraction of some disbelievers is entirely possible. Moreover, the creation of affection in Ayah 60 of Surah At-Tawbah is also from Allah and by His command. In reality, the Ayah under discussion is a monotheistic statement regarding the reconciliation of hearts, similar to the Ayah: «و ما رمیت اذ رمیت و لكنّ اللّه رمى » (And you threw not when you threw, but Allah threw). [559]

559) Anfal, 17

Messages

1- Hearts and the bond between them are in the hand of Allah. «و ألّفَ بین قلوبهم»

2- Unity, affection, and love are among the divine blessings that Allah has mentioned and conferred as a favor upon the people and the Prophet. «و ألّف بین قلوبهم»

3- More important than outward unity is the inner, heartfelt bond, «و ألّف بین قلوبهم» otherwise, the disbelievers are also outwardly united, but their hearts are scattered. «تحسبهم جمیعاً و قلوبهم شتىّ» [560]

4- People are the strength of the leader only when they are united; otherwise, they break the leader's back. «ایّدك بنصره و بالمؤمنین و ألّف بین قلوبهم»

5- The Arab tribes were afflicted by deep enmity toward one another before embracing Islam. «الّف بین قلوبهم... ما الّفتَ بین قلوبهم»

6- Wealth and status do not always bring affection. «لو أنفقت... ما ألّفت»

7- Creating love and bonds among the believers is a manifestation of Allah's Might and Wisdom. «و ألّف بین قلوبهم... عزیز حكیم»

560) Hashr, 14

Ayah

يَـٰٓأَيُّهَا ٱلنَّبِىُّ حَسْبُكَ ٱللَّهُ وَمَنِ ٱتَّبَعَكَ مِنَ ٱلْمُؤْمِنِينَ (٦٤)
O Prophet! Sufficient for you is Allah and those of the faithful who follow you. (64)

Key Points

It is also possible to interpret the Ayah in this manner: O Prophet! The support of Allah alone is sufficient for you and your faithful followers. Meaning, Allah helps both you and your followers. According to the first interpretation, the believers, under the shadow of Allah, are the supporters of the Prophet. According to the second interpretation, the believers, alongside the Prophet, are under Divine protection.

A group of Jews from Banu Qurayzah and Banu Nadir falsely offered help to the Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله. The Ayah was revealed (stating) that the believers are sufficient for you. Furthermore, this Ayah is an introduction to the command for Jihad in the following Ayah.

In the book Fadā’il al-Sahābah by Hafiz Abu Nu'aym, one of the scholars of the Ahl al-Sunnah, it is stated: The intent of "the believers" (al-mu'minīn) in this Ayah is Ali ibn Abi Talib علیهما السلام. [561]

If seeking help from people and being encouraged by their support is done with attention to the will of Allah, it does not contradict Tawhid (monotheism). Shirk (polytheism) occurs when one relies on others against the power and will of Allah. However, as long as we consider hearts, thoughts, and support to be a ray of the Divine Will, we remain within the sphere of Tawhid. "حسبك اللّه و من اتّبعك"

561) Al-Ghadir, Vol. 2, p. 51

Messages

1- Following accompanied by faith is valuable, not following without faith, nor faith minus obedience. "اتّبعك من المؤمنین"

2- Allah's support for the believers is conditional upon their following the Prophet. "حسبك اللّه و من اتّبعك من المؤمنین"

3- Some Muslims were not obedient and followers of the Prophet. "من المؤمنین" (This is if the intent of min is ba'ḍiyyah (indicating 'some'), and not bayāniyyah (explanatory) clarifying the word "اتّبعك").

Ayah

يَـٰٓأَيُّهَا ٱلنَّبِىُّ حَرِّضِ ٱلْمُؤْمِنِينَ عَلَى ٱلْقِتَالِ ۚ إِن يَكُن مِّنكُمْ عِشْرُونَ صَـٰبِرُونَ يَغْلِبُوا۟ مِا۟ئَتَيْنِ ۚ وَإِن يَكُن مِّنكُم مِّا۟ئَةٌ يَغْلِبُوٓا۟ أَلْفًا مِّنَ ٱلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا۟ بِأَنَّهُمْ قَوْمٌ لَّا يَفْقَهُونَ (٦٥)
O Prophet! Urge on the faithful to fight: If there be twenty patient men among you, they shall overcome two hundred; and if there be a hundred of you, they shall overcome a thousand of the faithless, for they are a lot who do not understand. (65)

Key Points

This Ayah negates the balance of power based on numbers and relies instead on morale, faith, and patience. To prevent the assumption that the victory of twenty men over two hundred men is an exaggeration, it reiterates that one hundred men will overcome one thousand men, provided they are believers and patient. In the battles of early Islam, there was never a statistical balance between the believers and the disbelievers. In the Battle of Badr, 313 men faced one thousand men; in Uhud, 700 Muslims faced 3,000 disbelievers; in the Battle of the Trench (Khandaq), three thousand men faced ten thousand men; and in the Battle of Mu'tah, ten thousand Muslims faced one hundred thousand disbelievers. Imam Sadiq عليه السلام said: In the beginning, a single believer was commanded to wage Jihad against ten polytheists and did not have the right to flee or turn his back. [562]

562) Kafi, Vol. 5, p. 69

Messages

1- One of the duties of the leader is to encourage people toward Jihad and fighting the enemy. (The role of the commander's speech before the operation). «یا ایّها النّبىّ حَرِّض»

2- Difficult tasks require encouragement, motivation, and instillation (of confidence). «حَرِّض»

3- Propaganda (or mobilization) is essential in war and Jihad. «حَرِّض»

4- The determining factor on the battlefronts is faith and steadfastness, not merely the number of personnel or the apparent balance of forces. «عشرون صابرون یغلبوا مأتین»

5- In the early days of Islam, the command for Jihad was fulfilled even when facing one-tenth of the enemy's forces. «عشرون صابرون یغلبوا مأتین» The Muslims of early Islam possessed such faith, knowledge, and certainty that their victory over ten times their number was definite and guaranteed. («یغلبوا» indicates the guarantee of victory.)

6- The soldier of Islam must possess three characteristics: faith, patience, and awareness. («مؤمنین», «صابرون», and concerning the disbelievers, He says: «لایفقهون».)

7- Allah's obligations are based on the intellectual and physical capabilities of man. (At one point in time, one person had to fight ten people, which this Ayah refers to, but at another point, one person faced two people, which is referred to in the next Ayah.)

Ayah

ٱلْـَٔـٰنَ خَفَّفَ ٱللَّهُ عَنكُمْ وَعَلِمَ أَنَّ فِيكُمْ ضَعْفًا ۚ فَإِن يَكُن مِّنكُم مِّا۟ئَةٌ صَابِرَةٌ يَغْلِبُوا۟ مِا۟ئَتَيْنِ ۚ وَإِن يَكُن مِّنكُمْ أَلْفٌ يَغْلِبُوٓا۟ أَلْفَيْنِ بِإِذْنِ ٱللَّهِ ۗ وَٱللَّهُ مَعَ ٱلصَّـٰبِرِينَ (٦٦)
Now Allah has lightened your burden, knowing that there is weakness in you. So if there be a hundred patient men among you, they shall overcome two hundred; and if there be a thousand, they shall overcome two thousand, by Allah’s leave; and Allah is with the patient. (66)

Key Points

The meaning of ḍa‘f (weakness) in the Ayah is weakness in faith and morale, because the army of Islam had not decreased in terms of numbers (‘iddah) or equipment (‘uddah). In this Ayah and the previous Ayah, three spiritual issues that are factors for victory—and whose absence leads to defeat—are mentioned: patience (ṣabr), faith (īmān), and awareness (āgāhī). Of course, the main role in victory belongs to the permission and will of Allah; otherwise, in the Battle of Ḥunayn, despite the large population, the Muslims were defeated and fled: «أعجبتكم كثرتكم ... ثم ولّیتم مدبرین» (563) The differing rulings in this Ayah and the previous Ayah relate to two different groups and different circumstances, and it does not mean that the first ruling was abrogated or invalidated. (564)

563) Tawbah, 25 564) Tafsir-e Nemooneh

Messages

1- In management, regulations must sometimes change due to changing circumstances and suitability for the audience, and this does not contradict decisiveness. «الآن خفّف»

2- Determining the duty and changing the law is solely in the hands of Allah. «خفّف اللّه»

3- Faith is subject to decrease and increase. «اَنّ فیكم ضَعفاً»

4- A large population does not always lead to strong morale; sometimes the population increases, but morale declines. «انّ فیكم ضَعفاً»

5- In legislation, attention must also be paid to the morale and capability of individuals. «خفّف اللّه عنكم و علم انّ فیكم ضَعفاً»

6- The main factor for defeat comes from within, not from outside. «فیكم ضعفاً»

7- Commanders must pay attention to the morale, vitality, faith, and resolve of the army of Islam. «علم أن فیكم ضعفاً»

8- Weakness of will sometimes reduces the combat and spiritual capacity from ten times to two times (i.e., an eighty percent reduction). «مائة یغلبوا مأتین» while the previous Ayah stated: «مائة یغلبوا ألفاً»

9- Muslims, even when their morale is weak, must still possess twice the strength of the disbelievers. «فیكم ضعفاً... ان یكن منكم ألف یغلبوا ألفَین»

10- Let us not be arrogant about our victory, for victory is by His will. «باذن اللّه»

11- The patient person is beloved by Allah, under His protection, and included in Divine assistance. «واللّه مع الصّابرین»

12- The slogan «واللّه مع الصابرین» (And Allah is with the patient) must be instilled on the battlefronts and wherever resistance and patience are needed. «واللّه مع الصابرین»

Ayah

مَا كَانَ لِنَبِىٍّ أَن يَكُونَ لَهُۥٓ أَسْرَىٰ حَتَّىٰ يُثْخِنَ فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ ۚ تُرِيدُونَ عَرَضَ ٱلدُّنْيَا وَٱللَّهُ يُرِيدُ ٱلْـَٔاخِرَةَ ۗ وَٱللَّهُ عَزِيزٌ حَكِيمٌ (٦۷)
A prophet may not take captives until he has thoroughly decimated [the enemy] in the land. You desire the transitory gains of this world, while Allah desires [for you] [the reward of] the Hereafter, and Allah is all-mighty, all-wise. (67)

Key Points

"Thukhn" (ثخن) means thickness, density, and heaviness. In warfare, it is a metaphor for firmness, victory, and clear, powerful dominance. This Ayah is a warning that taking captives, accepting ransom for their release, and collecting war spoils should not distract Muslims from the main objective, lest they suffer an unforeseen blow.

Messages

1- Determining the method of warfare and taking captives is among the duties of the Prophet. «ما كان لنبىّ...»

2- The tradition and practice (Sīrah) of the Divine Prophets was that they should not take captives until their base was firmly established. «ما كان لنبىّ... حتّى یثخن»

3- The danger of worldliness (Dunyā-garā'ī) must not be overlooked, even for believers, during the most critical military circumstances. «تریدون عرَض الدنیا»

4- On the battlefield, taking captives is forbidden before achieving complete dominance over the area. «ما كان... حتّى یُثخِن»

5- The duty of the combatant is first to stabilize his own position (not to take captives or collect spoils). «حتّى یُثخِن فى الارض»

6- Focus on the objective and avoidance of dispersion (Tashattut) in war is a necessity. «حتّى یثخن فى الارض»

7- The world (Dunyā) is unstable and transient. «عرض الدنیا»

8- Suppressing the opponents of religion and strengthening the foundation of Monotheism (Tawhīd) is seeking the Hereafter (Ākhirah). «حتّى یثخن فى الارض... واللّه یرید الاخرة»

9- The main goal in Islamic warfare and struggle (Jihād) is achieving benefits in the Hereafter (gaining Allah's pleasure, strengthening the truth, and striving for the salvation of the oppressed), not acquiring spoils, captives, or ransom. «واللّه یرید الاخرة»

10- Allah's guidance regarding matters of war is wise, and applying them is the key to honor and victory. «واللّه عزیز حكیم»

11- The religion of Islam is not only connected to social and worldly matters, but it has also intervened and given guidance even on the minutest tactical issues of war. (The entire Ayah)

Ayahs

لَّوْلَا كِتَـٰبٌ مِّنَ ٱللَّهِ سَبَقَ لَمَسَّكُمْ فِيمَآ أَخَذْتُمْ عَذَابٌ عَظِيمٌ (٦۸)
Had it not been for a prior decree of Allah, surely there would have befallen you a great punishment for what you took. (68)
فَكُلُوا۟ مِمَّا غَنِمْتُمْ حَلَـٰلًا طَيِّبًا ۚ وَٱتَّقُوا۟ ٱللَّهَ ۚ إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ غَفُورٌ رَّحِيمٌ (٦۹)
Avail yourselves of the spoils you have taken as lawful and good, and be wary of Allah. Indeed Allah is all-forgiving, all-merciful. (69)

Key Points

Regarding the meaning of the phrase, «كتاب من اللّه سبق» (A decree from Allah preceded), several interpretations have been stated: A: It refers to the Divine tradition that He does not punish anyone before stating the ruling. As He says in another Ayah: «وما كنّا معذّبین حتّى نبعث رسولاً» [565] B: It refers to the Divine Will regarding the victory of the Muslims in the Battle of Badr, for if Allah's decree regarding victory had not preceded, your taking of captives before securing your positions would have inflicted a heavy blow upon you. [566] C: Perhaps the intent is the same point we read in Ayah 33 of this very Surah: that as long as the Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله is present among you, Allah will not punish you. «وما كان اللّه لیعذّبهم و انت فیهم» (But Allah would not punish them while you, [O Muhammad], are among them.) Therefore, we interpret the Ayah thus: If it were not for your blessed presence, which was previously mentioned in the heavenly Book, the Muslims in the Battle of Badr would have committed a major sin and incurred a great punishment due to taking captives prematurely. According to narrations, the spoils (غنائم) in this Ayah refer to the amount received as ransom (fidyah) for freeing each captive, which ranged between one thousand and four thousand dirhams.

565) Al-Isra, 15 565) Tafsir Atayab al-Bayan

Messages

1- Taking captives in the heat of battle leads to defeat and severe punishment. «لمسّكم فیما اخذتم عذاب عظیم»

2- Dealing with spoils before the complete suppression of the enemy is forbidden. «لمسّكم فیما اخذتم عذاب عظیم»

3- The condition for consumption is that it must be lawful (halal), pure (tayyib), and pleasing. «حلالاً طیّباً»

4- Not all the spoils belong to you; rather, you must pay the Khums (one-fifth) of it. «فكلوا ممّا...» (So consume from what...) As we read in Ayah 41: «و اعلموا انّما غنمتم من شى ء فاِنّ لِلّه خمسه وللرسول...» (And know that whatever you take as spoils of war—a fifth of it is for Allah and for the Messenger...)

5- Be vigilant so that war spoils and the taking of ransoms do not change your noble motivation for Jihad, and always keep Allah in mind. «اتّقوا اللّه» (Fear Allah.) (Indeed, benefiting from spoils is a pitfall.)

6- The pardon of the punishment for taking captives prematurely in the Battle of Badr and the making lawful of the income derived from freeing the captives (even though you should not have taken them captive) is a manifestation of Divine Mercy and Forgiveness. «فكلوا... غفور رحیم»

7- Divine Forgiveness is a reflection of His Mercy. «غفور رحیم»

Ayah

يَـٰٓأَيُّهَا ٱلنَّبِىُّ قُل لِّمَن فِىٓ أَيْدِيكُم مِّنَ ٱلْأَسْرَىٰٓ إِن يَعْلَمِ ٱللَّهُ فِى قُلُوبِكُمْ خَيْرًا يُؤْتِكُمْ خَيْرًا مِّمَّآ أُخِذَ مِنكُمْ وَيَغْفِرْ لَكُمْ ۗ وَٱللَّهُ غَفُورٌ رَّحِيمٌ (۷٠)
O Prophet! Say to the captives who are in your hands, ‘If Allah finds any good in your hearts, He will give you [something which is] better than what has been taken away from you, and He will forgive you, and Allah is all-forgiving, all-merciful.’ (70)

Key Points

Regarding the occasion of the revelation (Shān Nuzūl) of the Ayah, it is narrated: Abbas, Aqeel, and Nawfal were taken captive in the Battle of Badr. The Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله freed them upon receiving ransom (fidyah), and they subsequently embraced Islam. The money paid for their ransom was later returned to them. [567] It is mentioned in the commentaries (tafasir) that after the Battle of Badr, some suggested that out of respect for the Prophet, ransom should not be taken from Abbas, His uncle. The Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله said: «واللّه لاتذرن منه درهماً» "By Allah, do not forgo even a single dirham of it!" Then the Prophet told His uncle: You are wealthy; pay the ransom for yourself and your nephew, Aqeel. Abbas said: If I pay the ransom, I will become penniless! The Prophet said: Spend from the money You left with your wife Umm al-Fadl in Mecca. Abbas said: No one knew about this matter; I realized that you are truly the Messenger of Allah. And he embraced Islam right there. In the Islamic system, three legitimate and acceptable plans exist for prisoners of war: 1. Releasing them without ransom, such as what happened during the Conquest of Mecca, even though the Muslims had the power to take captives and demand ransom. However, the Prophet of Islam صلى الله علیه وآله freed everyone with the statement «انتم الطلقاء» (You are the freed ones). 2. Releasing the captives by taking monetary ransom or through prisoner exchange. 3. Keeping the captives in the hands of the Muslims, both to prevent the enemy from gaining strength and so that they may gradually learn the teachings of the religion and be guided toward Islam. The choice of any of these three paths is at the discretion of the Islamic ruler.

567) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn

Messages

  1. The Prophet and the leader of the Islamic government are tasked with speaking to the captives and guiding them. «یا ایّها النبىّ قل لمن فى ایدیكم...»

  2. Treat the captives in such a way that the ground for their guidance and instruction is prepared. (The use of the words "khayr" [good] and "maghfirah" [forgiveness] for the defeated conveys this message.)

  3. Preaching and guiding prisoners of war is necessary, and every opportunity must be used for propagation. «قل لمن فى ایدیكم»

  4. True good (khayr) is embracing Islam and faith. «اِن یَعلمِ اللّه فى قلوبكم خیراً»

  5. Give people hope that with the slightest good (khayr) they hold in their hearts, they can attain felicity. «اِن یَعلمِ اللّه فى قلوبكم خیراً یؤتكم خیراً»

  6. The goal of war is the guidance of people and the breaking of tyranny (taghut), not killing, looting, taking captives, or demanding ransom. «یؤتكم خیراً مما اُخذ منكم»

  7. Even participation in war against the Noble Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله does not prevent repentance, return, and the reception of Divine forgiveness. «قل لمن فى ایدیكم... یغفر لكم واللّه غفور رحیم»

  8. Accepting Islam leads to the overlooking of errors and is a cause for receiving Divine grace and mercy. «اِن یَعلمِ اللّه فى قلوبكم خیراً... یغفر لكم واللّه غفور رحیم»

Ayah

وَإِن يُرِيدُوا۟ خِيَانَتَكَ فَقَدْ خَانُوا۟ ٱللَّهَ مِن قَبْلُ فَأَمْكَنَ مِنْهُمْ ۗ وَٱللَّهُ عَلِيمٌ حَكِيمٌ (۷۱)
But if they seek to betray you, then they have already betrayed Allah earlier, and He gave [you] power over them; and Allah is all-knowing, all-wise. (71)

Key Points

It is understood from the totality of this Ayah and the previous Ayah that the principle of reform (Iṣlāḥ), guidance (Irshād), and direction (Hidāyah) should not be ignored due to negative possibilities. Although the polytheists of Mecca committed various types of treachery against the Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله and the early Muslims for years, if the expediency lies in freeing the captives, the possibility of their treachery should not prevent the fulfillment of the duty. Yes, regarding the enemy captives, we should have neither absolute suspicion (sū’ ẓann) nor complete good faith (ḥusn ẓann), neither pure harshness nor mere compassion. Rather, we should deal with them with sharp intelligence, reliance (Tawakkul), decisiveness, kindness, and affection.

Messages

1- The possibility of the captives reverting (to hostility) and committing treachery is not an obstacle to their release. «ان یریدوا خیانتك»

2- Enemies have a history of treachery. «خانوا اللّه من قبل»

3- Allah grants dominance to the truth and its adherents. «فأمكن منهم»

4- Treachery leads to captivity and being brought under control. «خانوا... فأمكن»

5- Allah is aware of the enemies' intentions and is Wise (Ḥakīm) and judicious regarding the command He issues. «واللّه علیم حكیم»

Ayah

إِنَّ ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ وَهَاجَرُوا۟ وَجَـٰهَدُوا۟ بِأَمْوَٰلِهِمْ وَأَنفُسِهِمْ فِى سَبِيلِ ٱللَّهِ وَٱلَّذِينَ ءَاوَوا۟ وَّنَصَرُوٓا۟ أُو۟لَـٰٓئِكَ بَعْضُهُمْ أَوْلِيَآءُ بَعْضٍ ۚ وَٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ وَلَمْ يُهَاجِرُوا۟ مَا لَكُم مِّن وَلَـٰيَتِهِم مِّن شَىْءٍ حَتَّىٰ يُهَاجِرُوا۟ ۚ وَإِنِ ٱسْتَنصَرُوكُمْ فِى ٱلدِّينِ فَعَلَيْكُمُ ٱلنَّصْرُ إِلَّا عَلَىٰ قَوْمٍۭ بَيْنَكُمْ وَبَيْنَهُم مِّيثَـٰقٌ ۗ وَٱللَّهُ بِمَا تَعْمَلُونَ بَصِيرٌ (۷۲)
Indeed those who have believed and migrated and waged jihad with their possessions and persons in the way of Allah, and those who gave [them] shelter and help—they are heirs of one another. As for those who have believed but did not migrate, you have no heirdom in relation to them whatsoever until they migrate. Yet if they ask your help for the sake of religion, it is incumbent on you to help them, excepting against a people with whom you have a treaty; and Allah watches what you do. (72)

Key Points

In the thirteenth year of the Prophetic mission (Bi'tha), and after ten years had passed since the Prophet's public invitation to Islam, the polytheists of Mecca had still not ceased their persecution and harassment of the Prophet and the Muslims, and they were increasing its severity day by day. This continued until they decided, through a calculated conspiracy and a collective action, to assassinate the Prophet and eradicate Islam. When the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله learned of the conspiracy, and through the self-sacrifice of Ali عليه السلام, the preparations for the migration (Hijra) were made overnight. On the first of Rabi' al-Awwal, He first went to the Cave of Thawr and remained hidden there miraculously for three days. During this time, only Hadrat Ali عليه السلام provided Him with food and kept Him informed of the outside news. Finally, the Prophet, having advised Hadrat Ali to announce the migration of the Muslims to Yathrib and to bring His household (Ahl al-Bayt) with him, set off for Yathrib Himself. After traveling a distance of about four hundred kilometers, He reached the locality of Quba on the twelfth day of Rabi' al-Awwal, and the rest of the Muhajirun (Emigrants) joined them. The people of Yathrib gave the Emigrants a splendid welcome, accepted them with open arms, and changed the name of Yathrib to "Madinat al-Nabi" (City of the Prophet) and "Madinat al-Rasul" (City of the Messenger). Then, to prevent and avoid discord, the Prophet established a covenant of brotherhood (Aqd al-Ukhuwwah) between the Muhajirun and the Ansar (Helpers), who numbered about three hundred, pairing them up, and He founded the Quba Mosque. [568]

The Muhajirun are those who believed in the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله in Mecca, and because they were under pressure there, they abandoned their homes and belongings and migrated to Medina with the Prophet. The Ansar are the Muslims of Medina who welcomed the Prophet of Islam and the Muhajirun, gave them shelter among themselves, and supported them. In this Ayah, themes such as: the bond between the Muhajirun and the Ansar, the necessity of migration, disregard for the affluent who avoid migration, mutual commitment between the Muhajirun and the Ansar, and the rescue of imprisoned Muslims (provided it does not harm previous treaties), are discussed.

Usually, nations have set the most important and greatest event that occurred in their social, scientific, or religious life, and which they consider a turning point in their national history, as the starting point of their calendar. For example, the birth of Jesus Christ عليه السلام among Christians, or the Year of the Elephant (the year the army of Abrahah attacked Mecca and was destroyed) among the Arabs, was set as the beginning of the year. Since the religion of Islam is the most complete heavenly law and is simultaneously independent of other religions, Muslims did not base their year on the birth of Christ. Likewise, the Year of the Elephant, although it was also the year of the Prophet's birth, or even the day of the Prophet's mission صلى الله عليه وآله, was not set as the basis for the year. However, with the Prophet's migration to Medina, where an independent government was founded and Muslims were able to operate freely in one place, the Prophet personally established the Hijri calendar. [569]

Furthermore, the establishment of the Hijri year can be inferred from the phrase "مِن اوّل یوم" (from the very first day) in the Ayah: «لمسجد اُسس على التّقوى من اوّل یوم احقّ ان تقوم فیه» [570] (A mosque founded on piety from the very first day is more worthy that you stand therein), which concerns the building of the Quba Mosque, because the Prophet's first action upon migrating to Medina was the construction of the Quba Mosque. [571]

Indeed, if the Hijra had not occurred, Islam might have been confined and destroyed in that very location. Most of our calamities stem from being immersed in luxury and abandoning constructive migrations. If all the Muslim minds, specialists, and experts residing in Western countries were to migrate back to their own countries, it would both strike a blow against the enemy and strengthen Islam and Islamic countries.

Migration is not exclusive to the Noble Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله; rather, migration from an environment of polytheism (shirk), disbelief (kufr), and sin is necessary for preserving one's religion and faith, or for returning from disobedience to Allah. Just as Allah, in response to those who excused their corruption and commission of sin by blaming the unhealthy environment, saying: «كنّا مستضعفین فى الارض» (We were oppressed in the land), says: Was not Allah's earth spacious enough for you to migrate to another place? «قالوا الم تكن ارض اللّه واسعة» [572] Of course, considering the phrase "مستضعفین" (oppressed/powerless), it becomes clear that if one has the ability to enjoin good and forbid evil, one must undertake this important task, not abandon society, but rather reform it.

Imam Sadiq عليه السلام says: When Allah is disobeyed and sinned against in the place and land where you live, migrate from there to another place. «اذا عصى اللّه فى ارض انت فیها فاخرج منها الى غیرها» [573]

Similarly, migration for the purpose of acquiring knowledge and migration for the purpose of conveying what one has learned to others is also necessary. «فلولانفر من كلّ فرقة منهم طائفة لیتفقّهوا فى الدّین و لینذروا قومهم اذا رجعوا الیهم» [574]

Two migrations occurred in early Islam: one in the fifth year of the Prophetic mission, the migration of a group of Muslims to the land of Abyssinia (Habasha), and the other in the thirteenth year of the Prophetic mission, the migration from Mecca to Medina.

568) Tafsir-e Namuneh, Sirah Ibn Hisham, and Furugh-e Abadiyyat, Vol. 1, pp. 417 to 450 569) Furugh-e Abadiyyat, Vol. 1, p. 435 570) Anfal, 108 571) Al-Sahih min Sirat al-Nabi, Allamah Ja'far Murtada, Vol. 3, p. 40 572) Nisa, 43 573) Bihar, Vol. 19, p. 35 574) Tawbah, 122

Messages

  1. Islam, faith, and belief are not sufficient without effort and struggle (Jihad). The Muslims of early Islam were either emigrants (Muhajirun), or those who gave shelter to emigrants (Ansar), or strivers (Mujahidin), or supporters of the strivers. «انّ الّذین آمنوا و هاجروا و جاهدوا باموالهم و انفسهم... والّذین آووا و نصروا» (Different circumstances bring specific duties for everyone: for one, migration; for another, giving shelter to the emigrants.)
  2. The relationship of guardianship (Wilayah) is specific to those who are dedicated to migration, struggle (Jihad), and helping others. «اولئك بعضهم اولياء بعض»
  3. Allah has established the right of guardianship between the Muhajirun and the Ansar. «والّذین آووا و نصروا اولئك بعضهم اولياء بعض»
  4. Migration toward the Islamic society is a condition for guardianship (Wilayah), and whoever does not migrate has no Wilayah. «لم یهاجروا مالكم من ولایتهم...»
  5. In the Islamic society, there must be a distinction between the strivers and the emigrants, and the affluent who avoid migration. «آمنوا ولم یهاجروا»
  6. Do not help the one who does not migrate, nor seek help from him. (The word «ولایتهم» (Wilayah of them) can be interpreted in two ways: 1. Your guardianship and support for them, and 2. Their help and support for you.)
  7. Migration is sometimes more important than Jihad. «حتّى یهاجروا» (until they migrate) is used, and He did not say: «حتّى یجاهدوا» (until they strive).
  8. Former defaulters who wish to join the Muslims must be welcomed with open arms. «حتّى یهاجروا» (Yes, as soon as those who abandoned migration migrate, you must support them.)
  9. If Muslims in other regions come under the pressure of disbelief (kufr), their plea for help must not be met with indifference. «ان استنصروكم فى الدّین فعلیكم النصر»
  10. In relations with others, priorities must be considered. As long as the disbelievers adhere to their treaties with the Muslims, no action can be taken to support the Muslims under their dominion. «الاّ على قوم بینكم و بینهم میثاق»

Ayah

وَٱلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا۟ بَعْضُهُمْ أَوْلِيَآءُ بَعْضٍ ۚ إِلَّا تَفْعَلُوهُ تَكُن فِتْنَةٌ فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ وَفَسَادٌ كَبِيرٌ (۷۳)
As for the faithless, they are heirs of one another. Unless you do the same, there will be strife in the land and great corruption. (73)

Key Points

Three possibilities have been stated regarding the meaning of "إِلَّا تَفْعَلُوهُ" (Illā taf'alūh): A: If you do not act according to Allah's command regarding the relationship of wilāyah (guardianship/mutual support) among the believers, and instead maintain ties with the disbelievers, it will result in great corruption, because they (the disbelievers) are united while you (the believers) are scattered. B: If you disregard the Muslims who are under pressure, they might be massacred or abandon Islam. C: If you do not observe international treaties with the disbelievers while simultaneously supporting a number of Muslims, all the disbelievers will mobilize against you, and great corruption will ensue.

Messages

1- When the disbelievers are allies to one another, "وَالَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا بَعْضُهُمْ أَوْلِيَاءُ بَعْضٍ" (And those who disbelieve are allies of one another), if the Muslims lack mutual cooperation, they will fall into discord (fitnah) and corruption. "إِلَّا تَفْعَلُوهُ تَكُن فِتْنَةٌ"

2- Accepting the wilāyah (guardianship) and dominance of the disbelievers is a great corruption and discord (fitnah) on earth. "إِلَّا تَفْعَلُوهُ تَكُن فِتْنَةٌ"

3- If there is no strong bond of wilāyah among you Muslims, the disbelievers will unite and destroy you. "إِلَّا تَفْعَلُوهُ تَكُن فِتْنَةٌ"

Ayah

وَٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ وَهَاجَرُوا۟ وَجَـٰهَدُوا۟ فِى سَبِيلِ ٱللَّهِ وَٱلَّذِينَ ءَاوَوا۟ وَّنَصَرُوٓا۟ أُو۟لَـٰٓئِكَ هُمُ ٱلْمُؤْمِنُونَ حَقًّا ۚ لَّهُم مَّغْفِرَةٌ وَرِزْقٌ كَرِيمٌ (۷٤)
Those who have believed, migrated, and waged jihad in the way of Allah, and those who gave them shelter and help, it is they who are truly the faithful. For them shall be forgiveness and a noble provision. (74)

Messages

1- Faith precedes action. «آمنوا وهاجروا»

2- The value of deeds is realized when they possess a divine color and an ultimate motivation. «فى سبیل اللّه»

3- True faith manifests in migration (Hijrah), striving (Jihad), giving refuge, and aiding the striving Muslims. «و الّذین آوَوا ونصروا اولئك هم المؤمنون حقّاً»

4- Even the truly faithful are not immune from error and sin, and they require divine forgiveness. «المؤمنون حقّاً لهم مغفرة»

5- Migration and striving are factors for forgiveness and the bestowal of special divine provision (Rizq). «آمنوا و هاجروا و جاهدوا... لهم مغفرة و رزق كریم»

6- If you forgo meager provision for the sake of migration and striving, you will attain the noble and worthy divine provision (Rizq Karīm). «آمنوا و هاجروا و جاهدوا... لهم مغفرة و رزق كریم»

7- Man is always responsible; sometimes through striving (Jihad), sometimes through migration (Hijrah), sometimes through giving refuge to the migrants, and sometimes through supporting the combatants. (The entire Ayah)

Ayah

وَٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ مِنۢ بَعْدُ وَهَاجَرُوا۟ وَجَـٰهَدُوا۟ مَعَكُمْ فَأُو۟لَـٰٓئِكَ مِنكُمْ ۚ وَأُو۟لُوا۟ ٱلْأَرْحَامِ بَعْضُهُمْ أَوْلَىٰ بِبَعْضٍ فِى كِتَـٰبِ ٱللَّهِ ۗ إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ بِكُلِّ شَىْءٍ عَلِيمٌۢ (۷٥)
Those who believed afterwards and migrated, and waged jihad along with you, they belong to you; but the blood relatives are more entitled to inherit from one another in the Book of Allah. Indeed Allah has knowledge of all things. (75)

Key Points

One of the applications of the Ayah is inheritance, which, before Islam, was sometimes based on adoption and covenants, but Islam established it based on kinship and religiosity. Regarding the issue of Imamate and Caliphate, the Imams and scholars have repeatedly cited this Ayah, and in addition to arguing based on knowledge, history of struggle (Jihad), and piety, they have used the kinship of Imam Ali عليه السلام with the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله to prove the immediate succession (Imamate bilā faṣl) of that noble person. [575]

575) Tafasir Namuneh, Atyab al-Bayan

Messages

1- Former and early believers must consider those who will believe and migrate in the future as true believers, just like themselves. «والّذین آمنوا من بعد... فاولئك منكم» Yes, the embrace of the Islamic society is open to everyone, and being Muslim is not restricted to a closed or exclusive circle, although seniority can bring greater spiritual merits.

2- Believing relatives, in addition to the guardianship (Wilayah) of kinship over one another, also have a guardianship over each other under the shade of faith, migration (Hijra), and struggle (Jihad). «و الّذین آمنوا... واولوا الارحام بعضهم أولى ببعض»

3- In the Divine law, there is also a hierarchy among relatives. «و اولوا الارحام بعضهم أولى ببعض»

4- Migration (Hijra) is a condition for joining the true believers. (Migration is the key to the concentration and formation of one's own forces.) «هاجروا... فاولئك منكم»

«الحَمدُ لِلّه رَبِ ّ العالَمین»
